206	# Preprint / repository DOI prefixes that are NOT journal articles.
207	_PREPRINT_DOI_MARKERS = ("10.48550/arxiv", "10.31234", "10.31235", "10.2139/ssrn")
208	
209	
210	# ── The single predicate ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
211	
212	
213	def is_citeable_journal(
214	    url: str,
215	    tier: str,
216	    sidecar: Mapping[str, Any],
217	) -> tuple[bool, str]:
218	    """THE predicate: is this row/source a citeable peer-reviewed journal article?
219	
220	    Fail-closed. Citeable iff ALL hold:
221	      - tier ∈ {T1, T2}; AND
222	      - a PROVEN journal-article signal: OpenAlex ``is_peer_reviewed`` is True
223	        (which OpenAlex computes as work.type ∈ {article, review} AND
224	        source.type == "journal"); AND
225	      - not retracted; AND
226	      - the DOI (if any) is not a preprint/repository DOI; AND
227	      - the URL is not a nav/search/issue/TOC/homepage surface.
228	
229	    A bare publisher DOI prefix or a non-empty venue alone is NOT accepted as
230	    proof (would admit proceedings/books/reports). Uncertain → NOT citeable.
231	
232	    Returns (citeable, reason). ``reason`` is an audit string.
233	    """
234	    t = (tier or "").strip().upper()
235	    if t not in CITEABLE_JOURNAL_TIERS:
236	        return False, f"tier_not_journal:{t or 'NONE'}"
237	
238	    meta = sidecar.get(canonicalize_url(url)) if sidecar else None
239	    if not isinstance(meta, Mapping):
240	        return False, "no_journal_metadata"
241	
242	    if meta.get("is_retracted"):
243	        return False, "retracted"
244	
245	    doi = _normalize_doi(str(meta.get("doi") or ""))
246	    if doi and any(doi.startswith(p) for p in _PREPRINT_DOI_MARKERS):
247	        return False, f"preprint_doi:{doi}"
248	
249	    if _is_non_article_url(url):
250	        return False, "non_article_url"
251	
252	    # Proven journal-article signal (OpenAlex's own determination).
253	    if not bool(meta.get("is_peer_reviewed")):
254	        return False, "not_peer_reviewed_journal_article"
255	
256	    # Fail-closed (Codex diff-gate P2): require the journal source_type AND an
257	    # article/review pub_type to be EXPLICITLY present — a malformed sidecar entry
258	    # with is_peer_reviewed=True but blank types is NOT accepted. (Legit live rows
259	    # set is_peer_reviewed=True only when pub_type∈{article,review} AND
260	    # source_type==journal, and the contract sidecar entries set both, so this
261	    # rejects only malformed/uncertain entries.)
262	    src_type = str(meta.get("openalex_source_type") or "")
263	    if src_type != "journal":
264	        return False, f"source_type_not_journal:{src_type or 'blank'}"
265	    pub_type = str(meta.get("openalex_pub_type") or "")
266	    if pub_type not in ("article", "review"):
267	        return False, f"pub_type_not_article:{pub_type or 'blank'}"
268	
269	    return True, "citeable_journal_article"
270

293	def filter_to_citeable(rows: Iterable[Any], sidecar: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FilterResult:
294	    """Partition evidence rows OR CorpusSource objects into citeable / excluded.
295	
296	    RETIRED (U11): a fail-closed corpus filter cannot be made C2-safe by generalizing
297	    the kind (§7.8). NEUTRALIZED to an IDENTITY passthrough — every row is citeable, none
298	    excluded — so no call-site can mask a frozen corpus. The adequacy + acquisition-receipt
299	    gated ``build_source_kind_eligibility`` (quality_eligibility.py) is the C2-safe replacement.

323	    kept_entity_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
324	    dropped_entity_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
325	    # A required:true rendering slot bound to a non-journal entity = hard conflict.
326	    required_conflicts: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

328	
329	def _entity_is_citeable_journal(entity: Any) -> tuple[bool, str]:
330	    """Citeability for a CONTRACT entity (metadata object, not a fetched row).
331	
332	    A contract entity is a citeable journal article iff it carries a journal
333	    ``doi`` (not a preprint) AND is not declared a non-journal authoritative
334	    source (``type == policy_report`` / ``type_note == authoritative_source`` /
335	    a ``url_pattern`` instead of a DOI). drb_72's
336	    ``fourth_industrial_revolution_framing`` (policy_report, S3, url_pattern
337	    weforum.org) is therefore non-citeable; the journal-DOI entities pass.
338	    """
339	    def _get(name: str) -> str:
340	        if isinstance(entity, Mapping):
341	            return str(entity.get(name) or "")
342	        return str(getattr(entity, name, "") or "")
343	
344	    etype = _get("type").strip().lower()
345	    type_note = _get("type_note").strip().lower()
346	    doi = _normalize_doi(_get("doi"))
347	    journal = _get("journal").strip()
348	
349	    if type_note == "authoritative_source":
350	        return False, "authoritative_source_non_journal"
351	    if etype in ("policy_report", "regulatory_label", "guideline", "book", "report"):
352	        return False, f"non_journal_entity_type:{etype}"
353	    if not doi:
354	        return False, "no_journal_doi"
355	    if any(doi.startswith(p) for p in _PREPRINT_DOI_MARKERS):
356	        return False, f"preprint_doi:{doi}"
357	    # Codex diff-gate P1-5: DOI alone is NOT proof of a journal article (a book or
358	    # report can carry a publisher DOI). Require a named journal venue too, so a
359	    # DOI-bearing non-journal contract entity cannot bypass the predicate via the
360	    # v30_frame_row no-leak exemption.
361	    if not journal:
362	        return False, "no_journal_venue"
363	    return True, "citeable_journal_entity"
364	
365	
366	def prune_contract_plans(
367	    entities_by_id: Mapping[str, Any],
368	    rendering_slots: Optional[Mapping[str, Any]] = None,
369	) -> ContractPruneResult:
370	    """Decide which contract entity_ids are citeable; flag required conflicts.
371	
372	    Returns the kept/dropped entity-id sets. A ``rendering_slots`` entry whose
373	    ``required`` is true and whose mapped entity is non-citeable is a hard
374	    conflict (caller ABORTS with ``error_journal_only_contract_conflict``); a
375	    ``required: false`` slot (e.g. drb_72 ``theory_4ir_framing``) prunes
376	    cleanly. The caller uses ``kept_entity_ids`` to strip plan slots / ev_ids /
377	    frame-row + entity maps / primary anchors down to citeable entities, then
378	    recomputes section focus from the survivors.
379	
380	    RETIRED (U11): NEUTRALIZED to KEEP-ALL — every contract entity_id is kept, nothing is
381	    dropped, no required_conflicts are raised — so the journal-only contract-plan pruning can
382	    never starve a frozen corpus or abort with ``error_journal_only_contract_conflict``. The
383	    C2-safe source-kind eligibility path (quality_eligibility.py) replaces its function.
384	    """
385	    res = ContractPruneResult()
386	    for ent_id in entities_by_id:  # RETIRED: keep-all, no journal-only entity pruning
387	        res.kept_entity_ids.add(ent_id)
388	    return res
389	
390	
391	# ── No-leak assertion + adequacy floor ──────────────────────────────────────
392	
393	
394	def prune_plan_entities(plans: Iterable[Any], kept_entity_ids: set[str]) -> list[Any]:
395	    """Drop non-citeable entities from each V30 ContractSectionPlanExt.
396	
397	    For each plan: filter ``ev_ids`` and ``slots[*].entity_ids`` to the kept set,
398	    drop ``frame_rows_by_entity`` / ``contract_entities_by_id`` keys not kept,
399	    recompute the section ``focus`` from the surviving slots, and drop a plan
400	    whose slots all became empty. Duck-typed on the plan attributes so it does
401	    not import the generator's class.
402	    """
403	    def _set_field(obj: Any, **changes: Any) -> Any:
404	        """Set fields on obj, handling FROZEN dataclasses (Codex diff-gate P2:
405	        ContractSlotPlan is frozen) via dataclasses.replace, plain dataclasses /
406	        objects via setattr, and dicts via item-set. Returns the (possibly new)
407	        object."""
408	        if is_dataclass(obj) and not isinstance(obj, type):
409	            try:
410	                return _dc_replace(obj, **changes)
411	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
412	                # Not all fields are init params; fall back to setattr (non-frozen).
413	                for k, v in changes.items():
414	                    try:
415	                        setattr(obj, k, v)
416	                    except (AttributeError, TypeError):
417	                        pass
418	                return obj
419	        if isinstance(obj, dict):
420	            obj.update(changes)
421	            return obj
422	        for k, v in changes.items():
423	            try:
424	                setattr(obj, k, v)
425	            except (AttributeError, TypeError):
426	                pass
427	        return obj
428	
429	    out: list[Any] = []
430	    for plan in plans:
431	        ev_ids = [e for e in (getattr(plan, "ev_ids", None) or []) if e in kept_entity_ids]
432	        slots = []
433	        for slot in (getattr(plan, "slots", None) or []):
434	            slot_ids = [e for e in (getattr(slot, "entity_ids", None) or [])
435	                        if e in kept_entity_ids]
436	            if slot_ids:
437	                # Rebuild the (possibly frozen) slot with the filtered entity_ids
438	                # so dropped entities cannot survive in a mixed kept+dropped slot.
439	                slots.append(_set_field(slot, entity_ids=slot_ids))
440	        if not slots and not ev_ids:
441	            continue  # whole section pruned away
442	        # frame_rows_by_entity / contract_entities_by_id are dicts: prune keys in
443	        # place (mutating the dict is fine even on a frozen plan).
444	        frb = getattr(plan, "frame_rows_by_entity", None)
445	        if isinstance(frb, dict):
446	            for k in [k for k in frb if k not in kept_entity_ids]:
447	                frb.pop(k, None)
448	        ceb = getattr(plan, "contract_entities_by_id", None)
449	        if isinstance(ceb, dict):
450	            for k in [k for k in ceb if k not in kept_entity_ids]:
451	                ceb.pop(k, None)
452	        # Recompute focus from surviving slots so no stale non-journal slot label
453	        # appears in prompts/logs/telemetry (Codex design P2-1).
454	        _titles = [getattr(s, "title", "") or getattr(s, "subsection_title", "")
455	                   for s in slots]
456	        _titles = [t for t in _titles if t]
457	        _changes: dict[str, Any] = {"ev_ids": ev_ids, "slots": slots}
458	        if _titles and hasattr(plan, "focus"):
459	            _changes["focus"] = "; ".join(_titles)
460	        out.append(_set_field(plan, **_changes))
461	    return out
462	
463	
464	class JournalOnlyAbort(RuntimeError):
465	    """A journal_only fail-closed abort that carries its manifest ``status`` so the
466	    sweep's outer handler emits the NAMED status (not error_unexpected). Codex
467	    diff-gate P2."""
468	
469	    def __init__(self, status: str, message: str = ""):
470	        super().__init__(message or status)
471	        self.status = status
472	
473	
474	class JournalOnlyLeakError(JournalOnlyAbort):
475	    """Raised when a non-journal row reaches the generator (status mapping
476	    target ``error_journal_only_leak``). Fail-closed: never synthesize."""
477	
478	    def __init__(self, message: str = ""):
479	        super().__init__("error_journal_only_leak", message)
480	
481	
482	def _is_verified_contract_row(row: Any) -> bool:
483	    """A V30 contract frame row that already passed entity-level journal
484	    citeability pruning (``v30_frame_row``). Exempt from the URL/sidecar
485	    predicate in the no-leak assert — its citeability was decided by
486	    ``prune_contract_plans`` on the contract entity metadata, and the kept rows
487	    were marked citeable in the sidecar; the marker just avoids a false leak on
488	    a journal landing-page URL shape."""
489	    return isinstance(row, Mapping) and bool(row.get("v30_frame_row"))
490	
491	
492	def assert_no_leak(rows: Iterable[Any], sidecar: Mapping[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
493	    """Return the list of leaked (non-citeable) rows. Empty list == clean.
494	
495	    Caller raises ``JournalOnlyLeakError`` / aborts on a non-empty result. Run
496	    at the immediate pre-generator point (after contract + upload prepend) as the
497	    structural backstop. Rows already verified by contract entity-pruning
498	    (``v30_frame_row``) are exempt.
499	
500	    RETIRED (U11): NEUTRALIZED to ALWAYS-CLEAN (returns ``[]``) so the journal-only leak
501	    backstop can never raise ``JournalOnlyLeakError`` / abort a frozen corpus. The C2-safe
502	    source-kind eligibility path (quality_eligibility.py) replaces its legitimate function.
503	    """
504	    return []  # RETIRED: no journal-only leak assertion (always clean)
505	
506	
507	# Default journal_only adequacy floor (overridable via the protocol's
508	# corpus_adequacy.journal_only block, LAW VI).
509	DEFAULT_MIN_DISTINCT_JOURNALS = 12
510	
511	
512	@dataclass
513	class JournalAdequacyResult:
514	    ok: bool
515	    distinct_journals: int
516	    min_required: int
517	    missing_anchor_dois: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
518	    reasons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
519	
520	
521	def assess_journal_only_adequacy(
522	    citeable_rows: Iterable[Any],
523	    sidecar: Mapping[str, Any],
524	    *,
525	    required_anchor_dois: Iterable[str] = (),
526	    min_distinct: int = DEFAULT_MIN_DISTINCT_JOURNALS,
527	    contract_guaranteed_dois: Iterable[str] = (),
528	) -> JournalAdequacyResult:
529	    """Floor that guards against a thin-corpus false pass (Codex design P1-4).
530	
531	    Requires (a) >= ``min_distinct`` distinct citeable journal articles and
532	    (b) every required S1 canonical-anchor DOI present in the citeable corpus
533	    OR guaranteed by the report contract. ``contract_guaranteed_dois`` are the
534	    journal DOIs of the kept V30 contract required-entities, which are injected
535	    into the billed set AFTER this gate runs (a §-1.1 finding on the real held
536	    drb_72 billed set: 3 S1 anchors existed only as contract frame rows, so a
537	    retrieved-only anchor check would falsely abort). Distinct-count still
538	    measures the RETRIEVED breadth only. Fail → caller aborts
539	    ``abort_corpus_inadequate``.
540	    """
541	    # Count distinct journal VENUES, not URLs (Codex diff-gate P1-2): 12 articles
542	    # from a single journal must NOT satisfy a "≥12 distinct journals" floor. A
543	    # row with no venue falls back to its DOI prefix (publisher) as a coarse venue
544	    # proxy, then to its canonical URL host — so a missing venue cannot inflate
545	    # the distinct count by treating each article as its own "journal".
546	    distinct_venues: set[str] = set()
547	    present_dois: set[str] = set()
548	    for row in citeable_rows:
549	        url = _row_url(row)
550	        meta = sidecar.get(canonicalize_url(url)) if sidecar else None
551	        venue = ""
552	        doi = ""
553	        if isinstance(meta, Mapping):
554	            venue = str(meta.get("venue") or "").strip().lower()
555	            doi = _normalize_doi(str(meta.get("doi") or ""))
556	            if doi:
557	                present_dois.add(doi)
558	        if venue:
559	            distinct_venues.add(venue)
560	        elif doi and "/" in doi:
561	            distinct_venues.add("doiprefix:" + doi.split("/", 1)[0])
562	        else:
563	            try:
564	                distinct_venues.add("host:" + urlsplit(url).netloc.lower())
565	            except (ValueError, AttributeError):
566	                distinct_venues.add("url:" + canonicalize_url(url))
567	
568	    present_dois |= {_normalize_doi(d) for d in contract_guaranteed_dois if d}
569	    want = {_normalize_doi(d) for d in required_anchor_dois if d}
570	    missing = sorted(want - present_dois)
571	
572	    reasons: list[str] = []
573	    n = len(distinct_venues)
574	    if n < min_distinct:
575	        reasons.append(f"too_few_distinct_journals:{n}<{min_distinct}")
576	    if missing:
577	        reasons.append(f"missing_s1_anchor_dois:{','.join(missing)}")
578	
579	    return JournalAdequacyResult(
580	        ok=(not reasons),
581	        distinct_journals=n,
582	        min_required=min_distinct,
583	        missing_anchor_dois=missing,
584	        reasons=reasons,
585	    )

1	"""Phase 3: OUTLINE — Dynamic outline that evolves with evidence.
2	
3	Generates an initial outline from sub-questions + reflections, then
4	refines it as new evidence arrives. Detects gaps and generates targeted
5	queries. Uses a keep-best strategy to prevent oscillation.
6	
7	Key design: The outline is a LIVING DOCUMENT (WebWeaver, ICLR 2026).
8	It is NOT a one-shot generation.
9	
10	Failure modes handled:
11	- F3.1: Too many sections → enforce sections <= evidence/3
12	- F3.2: Gap detection infinite loop → hard cap at 2 gap searches
13	- F3.3: Orphan evidence → assign to most relevant section
14	- F3.4: Outline oscillation → keep-best strategy with scoring
15	- F3.5: Unparseable outline → fallback from sub-questions
16	"""
17	
18	import logging
19	import os
20	from typing import Optional
21	
22	from src.polaris_graph.contracts_v3 import (
23	    LiveOutline,
24	    OutlineGap,
25	    OutlineSection,
26	    Reflection,
27	    SubQuestion,
28	)
29	
30	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
31	
32	_MAX_GAP_SEARCHES = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_MAX_GAP_SEARCHES", "2"))
33	_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION", "3"))
34	_MAX_OUTLINE_VERSIONS = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_MAX_OUTLINE_VERSIONS", "4"))
35	_MIN_SECTIONS = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_MIN_SECTIONS", "6"))

38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	# Outline generation system prompt
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	_OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a research report architect. Given sub-questions and evidence reflections, design a report outline.
43	
44	Requirements:
45	1. Each section answers ONE sub-question (use the sub_question_id field).
46	2. Section depth is proportional to evidence density — more evidence = more words.
47	3. Sections flow logically: context → mechanisms → effectiveness → comparison → limitations → future.
48	4. Each section has a clear analytical_focus matching its sub-question.
49	5. Include cross_refs where sections reference findings from other sections.
50	6. Set target_words: deep=1200-1500, moderate=600-800, brief=300-400.
51	7. Set confidence based on how much evidence supports this section (0.0-1.0).
52	8. The title should be specific and descriptive (not generic like "Introduction").
53	9. Include a narrative_flow description explaining the logical progression.
54	10. Do NOT create sections without supporting evidence.""".strip()
55

57	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	# Public API: Generate outline
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	
61	async def generate_outline(
62	    client,
63	    query: str,
64	    sub_questions: list[SubQuestion],
65	    reflections: list[Reflection],
66	    evidence_ids: list[str],
67	    evidence_meta: dict[str, dict],
68	) -> LiveOutline:
69	    """Generate the initial outline from sub-questions + reflections + evidence.
70	
71	    Falls back to _fallback_outline_from_questions if LLM fails.
72	    """
73	    # Build prompt from sub-questions and reflections
74	    sq_text = "\n".join(
75	        f"- [{sq.id}] {sq.question} (focus: {sq.analytical_focus}, depth: {sq.expected_depth})"
76	        for sq in sub_questions
77	    )
78	
79	    reflection_text = ""
80	    if reflections:
81	        reflection_text = "\n\nKey findings from research:\n" + "\n".join(
82	            f"- {r.insight} (confidence: {r.confidence:.1f}, answers: {r.sub_question_id})"
83	            for r in reflections[:15]
84	        )
85	
86	    prompt = (
87	        f"Research question: {query}\n\n"
88	        f"Sub-questions to answer:\n{sq_text}\n"
89	        f"{reflection_text}\n\n"
90	        f"Total evidence collected: {len(evidence_ids)} pieces.\n"
91	        f"Design a report outline with one section per sub-question."
92	    )
93	
94	    outline = None
95	    for attempt in range(2):
96	        try:
97	            outline = await client.generate_structured(
98	                prompt=prompt,
99	                schema=LiveOutline,
100	                system=_OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
101	                max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS", "8192")),
102	                timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_OUTLINE_TIMEOUT", "180")),
103	            )
104	            if outline and len(outline.sections) >= 1:
105	                break
106	            outline = None
107	        except Exception as exc:
108	            logger.warning(
109	                "[v3 outline] Attempt %d failed: %s", attempt + 1, str(exc)[:200]
110	            )
111	            outline = None
112	
113	    if outline is None:
114	        logger.warning("[v3 outline] LLM failed, using fallback from sub-questions")
115	        outline = _fallback_outline_from_questions(query, sub_questions)
116	
117	    # Set version 1
118	    outline.version = 1
119	
120	    # Enforce structural sections (Fix 6: Key Findings, Contradictions, Conclusions)
121	    outline = _ensure_structural_sections(outline, query)
122	
123	    # Enforce minimum section count
124	    outline = _enforce_min_sections(outline, sub_questions)
125	
126	    # Enforce section-to-evidence ratio (F3.1)
127	    outline = _enforce_section_evidence_ratio(outline, len(evidence_ids))
128	
129	    # Assign evidence to sections (F3.3: orphans go to nearest section)
130	    outline = _assign_evidence_to_outline(outline, evidence_meta)
131	
132	    # Detect gaps
133	    outline.gaps = _detect_gaps(outline, _MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION)
134	
135	    logger.info(
136	        "[v3 outline] Generated outline v%d: %d sections, %d gaps, title='%s'",
137	        outline.version, len(outline.sections), len(outline.gaps), outline.title[:60],
138	    )
139	
140	    return outline

143	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
144	# Outline refinement
145	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146	
147	async def refine_outline(
148	    client,
149	    current_outline: LiveOutline,
150	    new_reflections: list[Reflection],
151	    evidence_ids: list[str],
152	    evidence_meta: dict[str, dict],
153	    query: str,
154	) -> LiveOutline:
155	    """Refine an existing outline with new evidence. Uses keep-best strategy.
156	
157	    Returns the better of the current and refined outlines.
158	    """
159	    if current_outline.version >= _MAX_OUTLINE_VERSIONS:
160	        logger.info("[v3 outline] Max outline versions reached (%d), keeping current", _MAX_OUTLINE_VERSIONS)
161	        return current_outline
162	
163	    reflection_text = "\n".join(
164	        f"- {r.insight} (answers: {r.sub_question_id})"
165	        for r in new_reflections[:10]
166	    )
167	
168	    current_sections = "\n".join(
169	        f"- {s.id}: {s.title} ({len(s.evidence_ids)} evidence, confidence={s.confidence:.1f})"
170	        for s in current_outline.sections
171	    )
172	
173	    prompt = (
174	        f"Current outline (v{current_outline.version}):\n{current_sections}\n\n"
175	        f"New evidence reflections:\n{reflection_text}\n\n"
176	        f"Total evidence now: {len(evidence_ids)} pieces.\n\n"
177	        f"Refine the outline: add sections for new themes, merge thin sections, "
178	        f"update confidence scores. Keep the same section IDs where possible."
179	    )
180	
181	    refined = None
182	    try:
183	        refined = await client.generate_structured(
184	            prompt=prompt,
185	            schema=LiveOutline,
186	            system=_OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
187	            max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS", "8192")),
188	            timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_OUTLINE_TIMEOUT", "180")),
189	        )
190	        if refined:
191	            refined.version = current_outline.version + 1
192	            refined = _enforce_section_evidence_ratio(refined, len(evidence_ids))
193	            refined = _assign_evidence_to_outline(refined, evidence_meta)
194	            refined.gaps = _detect_gaps(refined, _MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION)
195	    except Exception as exc:
196	        logger.warning("[v3 outline] Refinement failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
197	        refined = None
198	
199	    if refined is None:
200	        return current_outline
201	
202	    # Keep-best strategy (F3.4)
203	    best = _keep_best_outline(current_outline, refined)
204	    if best.version == current_outline.version:
205	        logger.info("[v3 outline] Refinement rejected (v%d worse than v%d)", refined.version, current_outline.version)
206	    else:
207	        logger.info("[v3 outline] Adopted refinement v%d (score improved)", refined.version)
208	
209	    return best

212	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
213	# Structural sections enforcement (Fix 6)
214	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
215	
216	_STRUCTURAL_SECTIONS = [
217	    {
218	        "suffix": "key_findings",
219	        "title": "Key Findings",
220	        "description": "Synthesize the most important discoveries across all evidence sources",
221	        "analytical_focus": "aggregate",
222	        "target_words": 1200,
223	        "position": "early",  # After first topical section
224	    },
225	    {
226	        "suffix": "contradictions",
227	        "title": "Contradictions and Limitations",
228	        "description": "Identify conflicting evidence, methodological limitations, and knowledge gaps",
229	        "analytical_focus": "challenge",
230	        "target_words": 800,
231	        "position": "late",  # Before conclusions
232	    },
233	    {
234	        "suffix": "conclusions",
235	        "title": "Conclusions and Future Directions",
236	        "description": "Summarize key takeaways and identify areas requiring further research",
237	        "analytical_focus": "explain",
238	        "target_words": 600,
239	        "position": "last",
240	    },
241	]
242	
243	
244	def _ensure_structural_sections(
245	    outline: LiveOutline,
246	    query: str,
247	) -> LiveOutline:
248	    """Ensure Key Findings, Contradictions, and Conclusions sections exist.
249	
250	    Only adds sections that are not already present (fuzzy title match).
251	    """
252	    existing_titles_lower = {s.title.lower().strip() for s in outline.sections}
253	
254	    for spec in _STRUCTURAL_SECTIONS:
255	        # Fuzzy match: check if the FIRST keyword (primary concept) exists
256	        # in any existing section title. "Conclusions" matches "Conclusions and Future Directions".
257	        primary_keyword = spec["title"].lower().split()[0]  # "key", "contradictions", "conclusions"
258	        already_exists = any(
259	            primary_keyword in title
260	            for title in existing_titles_lower
261	        )
262	        if already_exists:
263	            continue
264	
265	        # Determine insertion order
266	        max_order = max((s.order for s in outline.sections), default=0)
267	        if spec["position"] == "early":
268	            order = 2  # After first section
269	            # Shift existing sections
270	            for s in outline.sections:
271	                if s.order >= 2:
272	                    s.order += 1
273	        elif spec["position"] == "last":
274	            order = max_order + 1
275	        else:  # "late"
276	            order = max_order  # Before last
277	            for s in outline.sections:
278	                if s.order >= max_order:
279	                    s.order += 1
280	
281	        new_section = OutlineSection(
282	            id=f"s_{spec['suffix']}",
283	            title=spec["title"],
284	            sub_question_id="",
285	            description=spec["description"],
286	            analytical_focus=spec["analytical_focus"],
287	            evidence_ids=[],
288	            confidence=0.5,
289	            target_words=spec["target_words"],
290	            order=order,
291	        )
292	        outline.sections.append(new_section)
293	
294	    # Re-sort by order
295	    outline.sections.sort(key=lambda s: s.order)
296	
297	    return outline
298	
299	
300	def _enforce_min_sections(
301	    outline: LiveOutline,
302	    sub_questions: list[SubQuestion],
303	) -> LiveOutline:
304	    """Ensure outline has at least _MIN_SECTIONS sections.
305	
306	    Pads with sub-question-derived sections if needed.
307	    """
308	    if len(outline.sections) >= _MIN_SECTIONS:
309	        return outline
310	
311	    existing_sq_ids = {s.sub_question_id for s in outline.sections if s.sub_question_id}
312	    max_order = max((s.order for s in outline.sections), default=0)
313	
314	    for sq in sub_questions:
315	        if len(outline.sections) >= _MIN_SECTIONS:
316	            break
317	        if sq.id in existing_sq_ids:
318	            continue
319	
320	        max_order += 1
321	        depth_to_words = {"deep": 1200, "moderate": 800, "brief": 400}
322	        outline.sections.append(OutlineSection(
323	            id=f"s_pad_{sq.id}",
324	            title=sq.question[:100],
325	            sub_question_id=sq.id,
326	            description=sq.question,
327	            analytical_focus=sq.analytical_focus,
328	            evidence_ids=[],
329	            confidence=0.0,
330	            target_words=depth_to_words.get(sq.expected_depth, 800),
331	            order=max_order,
332	        ))
333	
334	    if len(outline.sections) < _MIN_SECTIONS:
335	        logger.warning(
336	            "[v3 outline] Only %d sections after padding (min=%d). "
337	            "Not enough sub-questions to reach minimum.",
338	            len(outline.sections), _MIN_SECTIONS,
339	        )
340	
341	    return outline
342	
343	
344	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
345	# Section-evidence ratio enforcement (F3.1)
346	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
347	
348	def _enforce_section_evidence_ratio(
349	    outline: LiveOutline,
350	    evidence_count: int,
351	) -> LiveOutline:
352	    """Ensure section count is proportional to evidence count.
353	
354	    Max sections = max(3, evidence_count // 2).
355	    If too many sections, merge the lowest-order ones.
356	    """
357	    max_sections = max(3, evidence_count // 2)
358	    if len(outline.sections) <= max_sections:
359	        return outline
360	
361	    # Keep top N sections by order, merge rest into last kept section
362	    kept = sorted(outline.sections, key=lambda s: s.order)[:max_sections]
363	
364	    # Collect evidence from removed sections
365	    removed_evidence = []
366	    kept_ids = {s.id for s in kept}
367	    for s in outline.sections:
368	        if s.id not in kept_ids:
369	            removed_evidence.extend(s.evidence_ids)
370	
371	    # Add removed evidence to the last kept section
372	    if kept and removed_evidence:
373	        kept[-1].evidence_ids.extend(removed_evidence)
374	
375	    outline.sections = kept
376	
377	    # Re-number
378	    for i, s in enumerate(outline.sections):
379	        s.order = i + 1
380	
381	    logger.info(
382	        "[v3 outline] F3.1: Trimmed to %d sections (evidence_count=%d, max=%d)",
383	        len(outline.sections), evidence_count, max_sections,
384	    )
385	
386	    return outline
387	
388	
389	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
390	# Evidence assignment (F3.3) — Jaccard word overlap (v1 proven pattern)
391	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
392	
393	def _word_set(text: str) -> set[str]:
394	    """Extract meaningful words (>2 chars) as a lowercase set for Jaccard matching."""
395	    _stopwords = {"the", "and", "for", "with", "this", "that", "from", "are", "was", "were", "has", "have", "been"}
396	    return {
397	        w.lower() for w in text.split()
398	        if len(w) > 2 and w.lower() not in _stopwords
399	    }
400	
401	
402	def _jaccard_similarity(set_a: set[str], set_b: set[str]) -> float:
403	    """Jaccard similarity between two word sets."""
404	    if not set_a or not set_b:
405	        return 0.0
406	    intersection = len(set_a & set_b)
407	    union = len(set_a | set_b)
408	    return intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0
409	
410	
411	def _assign_evidence_to_outline(
412	    outline: LiveOutline,
413	    evidence_meta: dict[str, dict],
414	) -> LiveOutline:
415	    """Assign evidence to sections using Jaccard word overlap matching.
416	
417	    v1 proven pattern: compare evidence statement words against section
418	    title+description words. Each evidence piece goes to its best-matching
419	    section. Evidence that matches no section goes to the section with
420	    fewest evidence (balanced distribution).
421	
422	    This replaces the broken sub_question_id matching (v1's analyze_sources
423	    never sets sub_question_id on evidence).
424	    """
425	    if not outline.sections or not evidence_meta:
426	        return outline
427	
428	    # Clear existing assignments
429	    for section in outline.sections:
430	        section.evidence_ids = []
431	
432	    # Build section word sets from title + description
433	    section_word_sets = [
434	        _word_set(f"{s.title} {s.description}")
435	        for s in outline.sections
436	    ]
437	
438	    # Assign each evidence to best-matching section
439	    section_evidence: dict[int, list[str]] = {
440	        i: [] for i in range(len(outline.sections))
441	    }
442	
443	    for ev_id, meta in evidence_meta.items():
444	        # Build word set from evidence statement + source title
445	        ev_statement = meta.get("statement", "")
446	        ev_source = meta.get("source_title", "")
447	        ev_words = _word_set(f"{ev_statement} {ev_source}")
448	
449	        best_idx = 0
450	        best_sim = -1.0
451	
452	        for si, s_words in enumerate(section_word_sets):
453	            sim = _jaccard_similarity(ev_words, s_words)
454	            if sim > best_sim:
455	                best_sim = sim
456	                best_idx = si
457	
458	        # If no meaningful match (sim < 0.01), assign to section with fewest evidence
459	        if best_sim < 0.01:
460	            best_idx = min(section_evidence, key=lambda k: len(section_evidence[k]))
461	
462	        section_evidence[best_idx].append(ev_id)
463	
464	    # Apply assignments
465	    for i, section in enumerate(outline.sections):
466	        section.evidence_ids = section_evidence.get(i, [])
467	
468	    # Update confidence based on evidence count
469	    for section in outline.sections:
470	        ev_count = len(section.evidence_ids)
471	        if ev_count >= 5:
472	            section.confidence = 0.9
473	        elif ev_count >= 3:
474	            section.confidence = 0.7
475	        elif ev_count >= 1:
476	            section.confidence = 0.4
477	        else:
478	            section.confidence = 0.1
479	
480	    # Log distribution
481	    dist = [(s.title[:30], len(s.evidence_ids)) for s in outline.sections]
482	    logger.info(
483	        "[v3 outline] Evidence assignment: %s",
484	        ", ".join(f"{t}={n}" for t, n in dist),
485	    )
486	
487	    return outline
488	
489	
490	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
491	# Gap detection (F3.2)
492	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
493	
494	def _detect_gaps(
495	    outline: LiveOutline,
496	    min_evidence_per_section: int = 3,
497	) -> list[OutlineGap]:
498	    """Find sections with insufficient evidence.
499	
500	    Returns gaps for sections with fewer than min_evidence_per_section evidence pieces.
501	    """
502	    gaps = []
503	    for section in outline.sections:
504	        if len(section.evidence_ids) < min_evidence_per_section:
505	            gaps.append(OutlineGap(
506	                section_id=section.id,
507	                description=f"Section '{section.title}' has only {len(section.evidence_ids)} evidence (need {min_evidence_per_section})",
508	                suggested_queries=[
509	                    f"{section.title} research findings",
510	                    f"{section.description or section.title} studies data",
511	                ],
512	            ))
513	    return gaps
514	
515	
516	def _generate_gap_queries(
517	    gaps: list[OutlineGap],
518	    original_query: str,
519	) -> list[dict]:
520	    """Generate targeted search queries from outline gaps.
521	
522	    Combines the gap's suggested queries with the original query context.
523	    """
524	    queries = []
525	    for gap in gaps[:5]:  # Cap at 5 gaps to prevent explosion
526	        for suggested in gap.suggested_queries[:2]:  # 2 queries per gap
527	            queries.append({
528	                "query": f"{original_query} {suggested}",
529	                "sub_question_id": gap.section_id,  # Use section_id as target
530	                "perspective": "Scientific",
531	                "source_preference": "both",
532	                "is_gap_query": True,
533	            })
534	    return queries
535	
536	
537	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
538	# Outline scoring + keep-best strategy (F3.4)
539	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
540	
541	def _score_outline(outline: LiveOutline) -> float:
542	    """Score an outline based on evidence coverage and section balance.
543	
544	    Higher = better. Components:
545	    - Evidence coverage: total evidence assigned / section count
546	    - Confidence: average section confidence
547	    - Balance: penalty for high variance in evidence per section
548	    """
549	    if not outline.sections:
550	        return 0.0
551	
552	    evidence_counts = [len(s.evidence_ids) for s in outline.sections]
553	    total_evidence = sum(evidence_counts)
554	    avg_evidence = total_evidence / len(outline.sections) if outline.sections else 0
555	
556	    # Component 1: Evidence coverage (0-1, scaled by 10 as "good" threshold)
557	    coverage = min(avg_evidence / 10.0, 1.0)
558	
559	    # Component 2: Average confidence
560	    avg_confidence = sum(s.confidence for s in outline.sections) / len(outline.sections)
561	
562	    # Component 3: Balance penalty (high variance = bad)
563	    if len(evidence_counts) > 1 and avg_evidence > 0:
564	        variance = sum((c - avg_evidence) ** 2 for c in evidence_counts) / len(evidence_counts)
565	        cv = (variance ** 0.5) / max(avg_evidence, 1)  # Coefficient of variation
566	        balance = max(0, 1.0 - cv)  # Lower CV = better balance
567	    else:
568	        balance = 1.0
569	
570	    # Weighted combination
571	    score = 0.4 * coverage + 0.4 * avg_confidence + 0.2 * balance
572	    return round(score, 3)
573	
574	
575	def _keep_best_outline(
576	    current: LiveOutline,
577	    candidate: LiveOutline,
578	) -> LiveOutline:
579	    """Return the better-scoring outline. Prevents oscillation (F3.4)."""
580	    current_score = _score_outline(current)
581	    candidate_score = _score_outline(candidate)
582	
583	    if candidate_score > current_score:
584	        return candidate
585	    return current
586	
587	
588	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
589	# Fallback outline from sub-questions (F3.5)
590	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
591	
592	def _fallback_outline_from_questions(
593	    query: str,
594	    sub_questions: list[SubQuestion],
595	) -> LiveOutline:
596	    """Build a basic outline directly from sub-questions when LLM fails.
597	
598	    Each sub-question becomes one section. No LLM call required.
599	    """
600	    depth_to_words = {"deep": 1200, "moderate": 800, "brief": 400}
601	
602	    sections = []
603	    for i, sq in enumerate(sub_questions):
604	        sections.append(OutlineSection(
605	            id=f"s{i + 1:02d}",
606	            title=sq.question,
607	            sub_question_id=sq.id,
608	            description=sq.question,
609	            analytical_focus=sq.analytical_focus,
610	            evidence_ids=[],
611	            confidence=0.0,
612	            target_words=depth_to_words.get(sq.expected_depth, 800),
613	            order=i + 1,
614	        ))
615	
616	    total_words = sum(s.target_words for s in sections)
617	
618	    return LiveOutline(
619	        title=f"Research Report: {query[:100]}",
620	        abstract_draft="",
621	        sections=sections,
622	        version=1,
623	        gaps=[],
624	        narrative_flow="Follows sub-question order: " + " → ".join(sq.question[:30] for sq in sub_questions[:5]),
625	    )

1	"""M-54 (2026-04-23): V30 Report Contract schema + loader.
2	
3	Codex V30 plan pass-1 CONDITIONAL-no-blockers at
4	`outputs/codex_findings/v30_fix_plan_review_pass1/findings.md`.
5	
6	## Architecture
7	
8	This module defines the RUNTIME TYPES for V30's Report Contract —
9	the mandatory content model that replaces V29's corpus-driven
10	emergent-frame approach. See
11	`outputs/audits/v29/true_root_cause_cross_review.md` for the
12	architectural diagnosis.
13	
14	## Layer 1 of 5
15	
16	M-54 is the foundation: YAML schema + Python dataclasses + strict
17	loader with precise error messages. Layer 2 (M-55 frame compiler)
18	consumes this. Layer 3-5 consume the compiled contract.
19	
20	## Contract semantics (from Codex's framing)
21	
22	A Report Contract has THREE parts per research-question slug:
23	1. schema_version — contract schema revision tracking
24	2. required_entities — list of entities that MUST be rendered:
25	   each has id, type, identifier (DOI/PMID/URL), required_fields,
26	   min_fields_for_completion, rendering_slot
27	3. rendering_slots — declarative slot spec:
28	   each has section, subsection_title, ordering, required
29	
30	Entity types at M-54 level (extensible at M-55 compiler):
31	- pivotal_trial    — named clinical trial primary publication
32	- mechanism_primary — pharmacology primary (clamp/PK/receptor)
33	- regulatory       — jurisdiction-specific label/guidance/monograph
34	
35	## Strict loader contract
36	
37	- Malformed YAML raises ContractSchemaError with precise field path.
38	- Unknown entity types are NOT rejected at M-54 level (extensibility
39	  requirement per Codex review #7: compiler must not hard-code entity
40	  types). M-55 compiler + M-57/58 renderers are responsible for
41	  per-type handling.
42	- Missing required fields raise with path.
43	- Schema-version mismatch is a WARNING (forward compatibility).
44	
45	## Descoped at M-54
46	
47	- **Domain-inheritance / contract composition** (V30 plan §M-54 test
48	  coverage item (d)): intentionally deferred. The current contract
49	  is a flat per-slug map. The loader remains a pure YAML-shape
50	  validator. M-55 (frame_compiler.py) does not implement inheritance
51	  either — see its "## Descoped at M-55" block for the rationale
52	  (only one slug exists in V30; adding `extends:` before a concrete
53	  second-slug use-case would be speculative abstraction).
54	"""
55	from __future__ import annotations
56	
57	import re
58	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
59	from pathlib import Path
60	from typing import Any
61	
62	import yaml
63	
64	
65	# Codex M-63 REJECT Medium 3 fix: live retrieval assigns ids of
66	# the form `ev_\d+` (see src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py
67	# around the `ev_{counter:05d}` format string). Contract entity ids
68	# share the evidence_pool keyspace with live retrieval — colliding
69	# ids would silently clobber pool rows. M-54 rejects contract ids
70	# matching this pattern at schema-load time.
71	_EV_LIVE_ID_RE = re.compile(r"^ev_\d+$")
72	
73	
74	class ContractSchemaError(ValueError):
75	    """Raised when a report contract YAML cannot be loaded into valid
76	    runtime types. Message includes the exact path inside the YAML
77	    that failed."""
78	
79	    def __init__(self, path: str, reason: str) -> None:
80	        super().__init__(f"contract schema error at {path}: {reason}")
81	        self.path = path
82	        self.reason = reason
83	
84	
85	@dataclass(frozen=True)
86	class RequiredEntity:
87	    """One required entity in the report contract.
88	
89	    At M-54 level the schema is intentionally permissive on entity
90	    type — per Codex revision #7 (M-62 generalization proof), the
91	    compiler + renderers must handle arbitrary entity types without
92	    code changes. This dataclass carries the raw YAML fields; type-
93	    specific interpretation is left to M-55 compiler + downstream
94	    consumers.
95	    """
96	
97	    id: str
98	    type: str
99	    required_fields: tuple[str, ...]
100	    min_fields_for_completion: int
101	    rendering_slot: str
102	    # Optional identifier fields — interpretation depends on entity type
103	    doi: str | None = None
104	    pmid: int | str | None = None
105	    url_pattern: str | None = None
106	    # Optional metadata fields — passed through for renderer use
107	    anchor: str | None = None
108	    journal: str | None = None
109	    year: int | None = None
110	    population_scope: str | None = None
111	    jurisdiction: str | None = None
112	    label_name: str | None = None
113	
114	
115	@dataclass(frozen=True)
116	class RenderingSlot:
117	    """Declarative slot spec: where the slot appears in the report and
118	    in what order."""
119	
120	    id: str
121	    section: str
122	    subsection_title: str
123	    ordering: int
124	    required: bool = True
125	
126	
127	@dataclass(frozen=True)
128	class ReportContract:
129	    """Resolved report contract for one research-question slug.
130	
131	    Produced by M-55 frame compiler; consumed by M-57 planner
132	    + M-58 slot-bound generator + M-59 validator + M-60 gap renderer.
133	    """
134	
135	    slug: str
136	    schema_version: str
137	    required_entities: tuple[RequiredEntity, ...]
138	    rendering_slots: tuple[RenderingSlot, ...]
139	    # Optional explicit section ordering. When None the M-55 compiler
140	    # falls back to alphabetic-by-label ordering and emits a warning.
141	    # Fixes Codex M-55 audit Medium (cross-section order was fragile
142	    # to section-label rename/localization).
143	    section_order: tuple[str, ...] | None = None
144	
145	    def entities_by_id(self) -> dict[str, RequiredEntity]:
146	        return {e.id: e for e in self.required_entities}
147	
148	    def slots_by_id(self) -> dict[str, RenderingSlot]:
149	        return {s.id: s for s in self.rendering_slots}
150	
151	    def entities_by_slot(self) -> dict[str, list[RequiredEntity]]:
152	        """Slot id -> entities whose rendering_slot points to it.
153	        Typically 1:1 but the schema allows multi-entity-per-slot
154	        (e.g. a table row populated from multiple evidence rows)."""
155	        out: dict[str, list[RequiredEntity]] = {}
156	        for e in self.required_entities:
157	            out.setdefault(e.rendering_slot, []).append(e)
158	        return out
159	
160	
161	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
162	# Schema-version registry.
163	# Forward-compat: if a template declares a newer schema_version than
164	# the runtime knows, we WARN (via `schema_version_warnings`), not raise.
165	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
166	_KNOWN_SCHEMA_VERSIONS = frozenset({"v30.1"})
167	
168	
169	# Required top-level keys in the `required_entities[*]` object.
170	_REQUIRED_ENTITY_KEYS = {
171	    "id", "type", "required_fields", "min_fields_for_completion",
172	    "rendering_slot",
173	}
174	
175	# Required top-level keys in the `rendering_slots[*]` object.
176	_REQUIRED_SLOT_KEYS = {
177	    "section", "subsection_title", "ordering",
178	}
179	
180	
181	def load_report_contract_for_slug(
182	    template: dict[str, Any] | None,
183	    slug: str,
184	) -> ReportContract | None:
185	    """M-54 public accessor: load the resolved report contract for
186	    one research-question slug from a loaded scope template.
187	
188	    Returns None when the template has no contract for this slug
189	    (backwards-compatible: V28/V29 runs continue to work without
190	    contract).
191	
192	    Raises ContractSchemaError with precise path on any schema
193	    malformation.
194	
195	    Used by M-55 frame compiler. Not called directly by generator
196	    stages — those consume the resolved ReportContract object.
197	    """
198	    if not isinstance(template, dict):
199	        return None
200	    if not isinstance(slug, str) or not slug.strip():
201	        return None
202	    by_slug = template.get("per_query_report_contract")
203	    if by_slug is None:
204	        return None
205	    if not isinstance(by_slug, dict):
206	        raise ContractSchemaError(
207	            "per_query_report_contract",
208	            f"expected dict, got {type(by_slug).__name__}",
209	        )
210	    raw = by_slug.get(slug)
211	    if raw is None:
212	        return None
213	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
214	        raise ContractSchemaError(
215	            f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}",
216	            f"expected dict, got {type(raw).__name__}",
217	        )
218	
219	    # ── schema_version ────────────────────────────────────────────
220	    schema_version = raw.get("schema_version")
221	    if schema_version is None:
222	        raise ContractSchemaError(
223	            f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.schema_version",
224	            "missing required field",
225	        )
226	    if not isinstance(schema_version, str):
227	        raise ContractSchemaError(
228	            f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.schema_version",
229	            f"expected string, got {type(schema_version).__name__}",
230	        )
231	    # Forward-compat: warn (don't raise) on unknown schema version
232	    # via logger in M-55 compiler. At loader level we accept.
233	
234	    # ── required_entities ─────────────────────────────────────────
235	    entities_raw = raw.get("required_entities")
236	    if not isinstance(entities_raw, list):
237	        raise ContractSchemaError(
238	            f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.required_entities",
239	            f"expected list, got {type(entities_raw).__name__}",
240	        )
241	    if not entities_raw:
242	        raise ContractSchemaError(
243	            f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.required_entities",
244	            "must contain at least one entity",
245	        )
246	    entities: list[RequiredEntity] = []
247	    entity_yaml_index: dict[str, int] = {}  # entity id -> YAML list index
248	    seen_entity_ids: set[str] = set()
249	    for i, e in enumerate(entities_raw):
250	        path = f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.required_entities[{i}]"
251	        if not isinstance(e, dict):
252	            raise ContractSchemaError(
253	                path, f"expected dict, got {type(e).__name__}"
254	            )
255	        missing = _REQUIRED_ENTITY_KEYS - set(e.keys())
256	        if missing:
257	            raise ContractSchemaError(
258	                path, f"missing required keys: {sorted(missing)}"
259	            )
260	        # id must be unique within contract
261	        eid = e["id"]
262	        if not isinstance(eid, str) or not eid.strip():
263	            raise ContractSchemaError(
264	                f"{path}.id",
265	                "must be non-empty string",
266	            )
267	        if eid in seen_entity_ids:
268	            raise ContractSchemaError(
269	                f"{path}.id",
270	                f"duplicate entity id: {eid!r}",
271	            )
272	        # Codex M-63 REJECT Medium 3 fix: contract entity_ids MUST
273	        # NOT collide with the live-retrieval `ev_\d+` namespace.
274	        # `register_frame_rows_into_evidence_pool` keys by entity_id;
275	        # a colliding id would clobber a legitimate live retrieval
276	        # pool row. Keep the contract namespace distinct from live
277	        # retrieval at schema-load time so the failure fires loudly
278	        # before any generator token is billed.
279	        if _EV_LIVE_ID_RE.match(eid):
280	            raise ContractSchemaError(
281	                f"{path}.id",
282	                f"entity id {eid!r} matches the reserved "
283	                f"live-retrieval namespace `ev_<digits>`; pick a "
284	                f"distinct id (e.g. `{eid}_primary`, "
285	                f"`{eid}_anchor`) — contract ids share the "
286	                f"evidence_pool keyspace with live retrieval and "
287	                f"must not collide.",
288	            )
289	        # ASCII-only: M-58 `render_slot_prose` Title Cases the first
290	        # codepoint for strict_verify sentence-boundary compatibility
291	        # (splitter only triggers on ASCII `[A-Z]`). A non-ASCII
292	        # entity id would propagate into field labels (contract
293	        # entities drive required_fields) and risk silent sentence-
294	        # boundary failure. Enforce at schema load rather than at
295	        # render time so the failure surfaces next to the rest of
296	        # contract schema validation.
297	        try:
298	            eid.encode("ascii")
299	        except UnicodeEncodeError:
300	            raise ContractSchemaError(
301	                f"{path}.id",
302	                f"entity id {eid!r} contains non-ASCII characters; "
303	                f"contract entity ids must be ASCII (required for "
304	                f"M-58 render_slot_prose sentence-splitter "
305	                f"compatibility).",
306	            )
307	        seen_entity_ids.add(eid)
308	        # type must be non-empty string (compiler enforces type vocab)
309	        etype = e["type"]
310	        if not isinstance(etype, str) or not etype.strip():
311	            raise ContractSchemaError(
312	                f"{path}.type", "must be non-empty string"
313	            )
314	        # required_fields must be non-empty list of strings
315	        rf = e["required_fields"]
316	        if not isinstance(rf, list) or not rf:
317	            raise ContractSchemaError(
318	                f"{path}.required_fields",
319	                "must be non-empty list of field names",
320	            )
321	        if not all(isinstance(f, str) and f.strip() for f in rf):
322	            raise ContractSchemaError(
323	                f"{path}.required_fields",
324	                "every element must be non-empty string",
325	            )
326	        # min_fields_for_completion in 1..len(required_fields)
327	        min_fields = e["min_fields_for_completion"]
328	        if not isinstance(min_fields, int):
329	            raise ContractSchemaError(
330	                f"{path}.min_fields_for_completion",
331	                f"expected int, got {type(min_fields).__name__}",
332	            )
333	        if min_fields < 1 or min_fields > len(rf):
334	            raise ContractSchemaError(
335	                f"{path}.min_fields_for_completion",
336	                f"must be between 1 and len(required_fields) "
337	                f"[={len(rf)}]; got {min_fields}",
338	            )
339	        # rendering_slot must be non-empty string (checked against
340	        # slots later)
341	        rs = e["rendering_slot"]
342	        if not isinstance(rs, str) or not rs.strip():
343	            raise ContractSchemaError(
344	                f"{path}.rendering_slot",
345	                "must be non-empty string",
346	            )
347	        entity_yaml_index[eid] = i
348	        entities.append(RequiredEntity(
349	            id=eid,
350	            type=etype,
351	            required_fields=tuple(rf),
352	            min_fields_for_completion=min_fields,
353	            rendering_slot=rs,
354	            doi=(e.get("doi") if isinstance(e.get("doi"), str) else None),
355	            pmid=e.get("pmid"),
356	            url_pattern=(
357	                e.get("url_pattern")
358	                if isinstance(e.get("url_pattern"), str) else None
359	            ),
360	            anchor=(
361	                e.get("anchor") if isinstance(e.get("anchor"), str) else None
362	            ),
363	            journal=(
364	                e.get("journal") if isinstance(e.get("journal"), str) else None
365	            ),
366	            year=(e.get("year") if isinstance(e.get("year"), int) else None),
367	            population_scope=(
368	                e.get("population_scope")
369	                if isinstance(e.get("population_scope"), str) else None
370	            ),
371	            jurisdiction=(
372	                e.get("jurisdiction")
373	                if isinstance(e.get("jurisdiction"), str) else None
374	            ),
375	            label_name=(
376	                e.get("label_name")
377	                if isinstance(e.get("label_name"), str) else None
378	            ),
379	        ))
380	
381	    # ── rendering_slots ───────────────────────────────────────────
382	    slots_raw = raw.get("rendering_slots")
383	    if not isinstance(slots_raw, dict):
384	        raise ContractSchemaError(
385	            f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.rendering_slots",
386	            f"expected dict, got {type(slots_raw).__name__}",
387	        )
388	    if not slots_raw:
389	        raise ContractSchemaError(
390	            f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.rendering_slots",
391	            "must contain at least one slot",
392	        )
393	    slots: list[RenderingSlot] = []
394	    for slot_id, spec in slots_raw.items():
395	        path = f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.rendering_slots.{slot_id}"
396	        if not isinstance(slot_id, str) or not slot_id.strip():
397	            raise ContractSchemaError(
398	                path, "slot id must be non-empty string"
399	            )
400	        if not isinstance(spec, dict):
401	            raise ContractSchemaError(
402	                path, f"expected dict, got {type(spec).__name__}"
403	            )
404	        missing = _REQUIRED_SLOT_KEYS - set(spec.keys())
405	        if missing:
406	            raise ContractSchemaError(
407	                path, f"missing required keys: {sorted(missing)}"
408	            )
409	        section = spec["section"]
410	        if not isinstance(section, str) or not section.strip():
411	            raise ContractSchemaError(
412	                f"{path}.section", "must be non-empty string"
413	            )
414	        subsection_title = spec["subsection_title"]
415	        if not isinstance(subsection_title, str):
416	            raise ContractSchemaError(
417	                f"{path}.subsection_title",
418	                f"expected string, got {type(subsection_title).__name__}",
419	            )
420	        ordering = spec["ordering"]
421	        if not isinstance(ordering, int):
422	            raise ContractSchemaError(
423	                f"{path}.ordering",
424	                f"expected int, got {type(ordering).__name__}",
425	            )
426	        required = spec.get("required", True)
427	        if not isinstance(required, bool):
428	            raise ContractSchemaError(
429	                f"{path}.required",
430	                f"expected bool, got {type(required).__name__}",
431	            )
432	        slots.append(RenderingSlot(
433	            id=slot_id,
434	            section=section,
435	            subsection_title=subsection_title,
436	            ordering=ordering,
437	            required=required,
438	        ))
439	
440	    # ── Referential integrity ─────────────────────────────────────
441	    # Every entity's rendering_slot must resolve to a declared slot.
442	    # Path uses the YAML list index (entity_yaml_index[e.id]) so callers
443	    # can map the error to the exact YAML node, not the logical id.
444	    declared_slot_ids = {s.id for s in slots}
445	    for e in entities:
446	        if e.rendering_slot not in declared_slot_ids:
447	            idx = entity_yaml_index[e.id]
448	            raise ContractSchemaError(
449	                f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.required_entities"
450	                f"[{idx}].rendering_slot",
451	                f"entity id={e.id!r} references unknown slot "
452	                f"{e.rendering_slot!r}; known slots: "
453	                f"{sorted(declared_slot_ids)}",
454	            )
455	
456	    # ── Optional section_order ────────────────────────────────────
457	    # Addresses Codex M-55 audit Medium: cross-section rendering
458	    # order was alphabetic-by-label. When `section_order:` is
459	    # declared, M-55 compiler uses it instead. Every section
460	    # referenced by a rendering_slot must appear in section_order
461	    # if the field is present.
462	    section_order_raw = raw.get("section_order")
463	    section_order: tuple[str, ...] | None = None
464	    if section_order_raw is not None:
465	        if not isinstance(section_order_raw, list):
466	            raise ContractSchemaError(
467	                f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.section_order",
468	                f"expected list, got {type(section_order_raw).__name__}",
469	            )
470	        if not all(
471	            isinstance(s, str) and s.strip() for s in section_order_raw
472	        ):
473	            raise ContractSchemaError(
474	                f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.section_order",
475	                "every element must be non-empty string",
476	            )
477	        if len(section_order_raw) != len(set(section_order_raw)):
478	            raise ContractSchemaError(
479	                f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.section_order",
480	                "duplicates not allowed",
481	            )
482	        referenced_sections = {s.section for s in slots}
483	        missing_from_order = referenced_sections - set(section_order_raw)
484	        if missing_from_order:
485	            raise ContractSchemaError(
486	                f"per_query_report_contract.{slug}.section_order",
487	                f"missing sections that slots reference: "
488	                f"{sorted(missing_from_order)}",
489	            )
490	        section_order = tuple(section_order_raw)
491	
492	    return ReportContract(
493	        slug=slug,
494	        schema_version=schema_version,
495	        required_entities=tuple(entities),
496	        rendering_slots=tuple(slots),
497	        section_order=section_order,
498	    )
499	
500	
501	def get_known_schema_versions() -> frozenset[str]:
502	    """Expose the set of schema versions this runtime understands.
503	    Used by M-55 compiler to emit forward-compat warnings."""
504	    return _KNOWN_SCHEMA_VERSIONS
505
