1	"""I-ready-007 (#1072): deterministic input harm-refusal classifier (offline, no model, §8.4-safe).
2	
3	POLARIS had NO input harm-refusal layer: a harmful-but-answerable, clinically-shaped query (e.g.
4	"optimal insulin dose to fatally harm an adult") passed the scope gate and yielded a FAITHFUL,
5	well-cited, audit-bundled report assisting the harmful objective. This module is the input guardrail.
6	
7	DESIGN — a HIGH-PRECISION floor, NOT a complete guardrail:
8	  * It keys on an explicit harm-INTENT / weaponization-ACTION clause, NOT on clinical / policy /
9	    epidemiology subject matter. "insulin dose for an adult with type 1 diabetes",
10	    "management of warfarin overdose", "the Chemical Weapons Convention verification regime", and
11	    "fentanyl overdose mortality statistics" all carry NO intent clause -> harmful=False. The clinical
12	    / policy product is never over-refused.
13	  * It catches EXPLICIT harm-intent: an intent-to-kill/poison clause against a person, a make/build/
14	    synthesize weaponization clause, CSAM, suicide-METHOD-seeking, illicit-drug-SYNTHESIS.
15	  * It is honestly a FLOOR: paraphrased / obfuscated / novel harm phrasing is OUT OF SCOPE and is
16	    deferred to a follow-up (a robust open-weight intent classifier). A keyword classifier cannot be
17	    complete (the #1082 clinical-router lesson); here incompleteness = under-refusal, so this is
18	    scoped as "strictly better than the current zero-layer", not "safe by completeness".
19	
20	FAIL-OPEN (Codex brief-gate decision exception_polarity=fail_open_proceed): any internal error returns
21	harmful=False so the caller proceeds to the normal path (a safety-classifier bug must never silently
22	abort a legitimate clinical run; the downstream faithfulness gate still bounds hallucination, and the
23	flag is OFF in the locked benchmark). The operator's "refuse when uncertain" lean is honored for
24	explicit category hits (which always refuse), NOT for ambiguous clinical subjects (over-refusing those
25	would break the product).
26	
27	The caller wires this behind ``PG_USE_SAFETY_REFUSAL`` (default OFF -> byte-identical). On harmful=True
28	the caller emits manifest status ``abort_safety_refused`` + a refuse-with-redirection message (2026
29	partial-compliance best practice, arXiv 2506.00195) BEFORE any retrieval or generator spend.
30	"""
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import re
34	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
35	
36	
37	@dataclass
38	class HarmDecision:
39	    """The harm-refusal decision. ``reasons`` is auditable telemetry for the manifest."""
40	
41	    harmful: bool
42	    category: str            # "" when harmful is False; else the matched category
43	    confidence: float        # 0.0 - 1.0
44	    reasons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
45	
46	
47	# A person/victim object — the lethal-intent patterns anchor to a PERSON so "kill a process",
48	# "kill the pain", "kill time" never match. Includes generic + clinical-victim nouns. The bare
49	# pronouns carry a trailing \b so a REFLEXIVE ("themselves"/"himself"/"herself") does NOT match —
50	# reflexive self-kill is self-harm semantics (its own category + help-guard), not lethal-against-
51	# others, so "support someone who wants to kill themselves" is a HELP query, not a harm request.
52	_PERSON = (
53	    r"(?:someone|some\s?one|a\s+person|a\s+human\s+being|a\s+human\b|a\s+man|a\s+woman|a\s+child|"
54	    r"an\s+adult|people\b|humans\b|him\b|her\b|them\b|"
55	    # "my/the/a X" is anchored to a PERSON noun (Codex diff-gate iter-3 over-refusal: bare "my \w+"
56	    # matched "kill my time"/"my process"). Whitelisted relations / roles (Codex iter-4: broadened).
57	    r"(?:my|the|a|an|his|her|their|your)\s+(?:roommate|boss|coworker|co-worker|colleague|wife|husband|"
58	    r"spouse|partner|ex|girlfriend|boyfriend|mother|father|mom|dad|parent|sister|brother|sibling|son|"
59	    r"daughter|child|kid|baby|infant|toddler|newborn|friend|neighbou?r|enemy|rival|teacher|landlord|"
60	    r"tenant|patient|in-?law|family\s+member|relative|grand(?:mother|father|ma|pa|parent|son|daughter)|"
61	    r"aunt|uncle|cousin|niece|nephew|nurse|doctor|physician|boss|coworker|victim|elderly\s+\w+)|"
62	    r"the\s+(?:patient|victim|person|target|elderly|baby|infant)|"
63	    # an adjective-qualified patient / resident ("a hospital patient", "an elderly resident").
64	    r"(?:my|the|a|an)\s+(?:hospital\w*|hospitali[sz]ed|elderly|\bicu\b|nursing[-\s]home|dialysis|"
65	    r"comatose|sedated|sleeping|unconscious|vulnerable|disabled|bedridden|terminally\s+ill)\s+"
66	    r"(?:patient|resident|person|relative))"
67	    # NOTE: a capitalized-given-name branch was REMOVED at iter-5 — it caught capitalized
68	    # pathogens/species ("kill Candida auris", "kill Salmonella"), over-refusing legitimate clinical
69	    # microbiology. Named-victim coverage ("kill John") is a documented FLOOR gap (under-refusal),
70	    # deferred to the robust classifier; over-refusing real science is the unacceptable direction.
71	)
72	
73	# Care / clinical-management / prevention FRAME for the LETHAL category — a query ABOUT treating or
74	# preventing harm, not causing it. Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1: bare care TOKENS ("treating", "ICU",
75	# "hospital") let an explicit kill-intent bypass ("...would kill the patient I am treating"). This now
76	# requires a genuine HELPING STRUCTURE (management/treatment OF, emergency/ICU/hospital MANAGEMENT,
77	# antidote/first-aid/...), AND the classifier additionally requires this frame to LEAD the kill clause
78	# (see _frame_leads) so a TRAILING incidental care word can never rescue a harm request.
79	_LETHAL_CARE_GUARD = re.compile(
80	    # Genuine clinical-management GOVERNING structures only (Codex diff-gate iter-3: a loose leading
81	    # "prevent"/"how to help" let "to prevent detection, what dose would kill my roommate" / "how to
82	    # help my friend to poison someone" bypass). Each form GOVERNS an overdose/poisoning/harm object.
83	    r"\b(?:manage|management|managing|treat(?:ment|ing)?|care)\s+(?:of|for)\b"
84	    r"|\b(?:emergency|clinical|hospital|\bicu\b|inpatient|paramedic|medical|prehospital)\s+"
85	    r"(?:management|treatment|care|protocol|guideline)"
86	    r"|\b(?:antidote|reversal|reverse|resuscitat\w*|first\s+aid|supportive\s+care|poison\s+control|"
87	    r"overdose\s+management)\b"
88	    # Codex diff-gate iter-4: clinicians/hospitals managing an overdose is a HELP query.
89	    r"|\b(?:hospitals?|clinicians?|doctors?|physicians?|nurses?|paramedics?|providers?|clinics?|"
90	    r"\bed\b|\ber\b|emergency\s+depart\w*|icus?|staff)\s+(?:\w+\s+){0,2}?"
91	    r"(?:manage|managing|treat|treating|handle|handling|respond|responding|care\s+for|stabili[sz])"
92	    r"|\bprevent\w*\s+(?:\w+\s+){0,3}?(?:death|deaths|overdose|poisoning|self[-\s]?harm|suicide|"
93	    r"from\s+(?:dying|overdosing))\b",
94	    re.IGNORECASE,
95	)
96	
97	# 1) LETHAL INTENT AGAINST PERSONS — requires an explicit intent-to-kill/poison clause toward a
98	#    person. Bare "drug + dose" carries no intent verb -> never matches. Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1:
99	#    the intent verb may be "would/will/that-would kill", not only "to kill" ("what dose of insulin
100	#    WOULD KILL my roommate" was missed).
101	_LETHAL_INTENT = re.compile(
102	    # (a) kill/poison/murder A PERSON, with any leading modal/infinitive/imperative. Codex diff-gate
103	    #     iter-3: "can/could", "how do/can I kill", "poison my coworker" were missed.
104	    r"\b(?:to|would|will|can|could|should)\s+"
105	    r"(?:kill|murder|poison|fatally\s+harm|cause\s+(?:the\s+)?death\s+of|end\s+the\s+life\s+of|"
106	    r"euthani[sz]e\s+(?!myself))\s+" + _PERSON
107	    + r"|\bhow\s+(?:to|do\s+i|can\s+i|would\s+i|should\s+i)\s+(?:kill|murder|poison|fatally\s+harm)\s+"
108	    + _PERSON
109	    # (b) lethal/fatal DOSE framed at a PERSON — anchored to _PERSON so "dose that would kill bacteria /
110	    #     tumor cells / norovirus" (legitimate science) does NOT match (Codex iter-3 over-refusal).
111	    + r"|\b(?:lethal|fatal|deadly)\s+(?:dose|amount|quantity)\b.{0,30}?\b(?:to|for)\s+" + _PERSON
112	    + r"|\b(?:overdose|dose|amount|quantity|how\s+much)\b.{0,45}?"
113	    r"\b(?:to|would|will|that\s+(?:would|will|could|can)|can|could|be\s+(?:lethal|fatal)\s+(?:to|for))\s+"
114	    r"(?:kill|murder|fatally\s+harm|cause\s+(?:the\s+)?death)\s+" + _PERSON
115	    + r"|\b(?:would|will|could|is|are)\s+(?:be\s+)?(?:lethal|fatal|deadly)\s+(?:to|for)\s+" + _PERSON
116	    # (c) explicit covert-kill markers.
117	    + r"|\b(?:poison|kill|murder)\s+" + _PERSON + r"\s+(?:undetectabl|untraceabl|without\s+(?:getting\s+"
118	    r"caught|detection|being\s+caught|a\s+trace))"
119	    r"|\bundetectable\s+poison|\buntraceable\s+(?:poison|method)\b"
120	    r"|\b(?:poison|toxin|substance|drug|chemical)\b.{0,30}?\b(?:undetectable|untraceable|undetected|"
121	    r"won['’]t\s+show|not\s+detected)\b.{0,20}?\b(?:autopsy|toxicology|post[-\s]?mortem|tox\s+screen)\b"
122	    r"|\bundetectable\s+(?:in\s+)?(?:an?\s+)?(?:autopsy|toxicology|post[-\s]?mortem)\b"
123	    r"|\bso\s+(?:that\s+)?(?:it|they|he|she|the\s+(?:patient|person|victim|baby|infant))\s+"
124	    r"(?:dies|die|will\s+die|won['’]t\s+survive)",
125	    re.IGNORECASE,
126	)
127	
128	# An explicit NON-PERSON kill target — cells / microbes / pests / crops / in-vitro. Codex diff-gate
129	# iter-3 over-refusal: "what dose would kill bacteria / tumor cells / norovirus" is legitimate science.
130	# When present, the generic (un-targeted) lethal branch is vetoed.
131	_NONPERSON_TARGET = re.compile(
132	    r"\b(?:bacteri\w*|cells?|cellular|tumou?rs?|cancer|viral|virus(?:es)?|norovirus|pathogen\w*|"
133	    r"microb\w*|germs?|fung\w*|mold|mould|weeds?|pests?|insects?|larvae?|parasit\w*|amoeba\w*|"
134	    r"yeast|spores?|tissues?|crops?|plants?|rodents?|vermin|microorganism\w*|mosquito\w*|"
135	    r"in\s+(?:a\s+)?(?:culture|vitro|the\s+petri))\b",
136	    re.IGNORECASE,
137	)
138	
139	# Generic un-targeted lethal intent ("overdose protocol TO CAUSE DEATH") — a human victim is implied.
140	# Only fires when NO explicit non-person target is present (so legit science is not over-refused).
141	# Excludes bare "lethal dose" (dual-use toxicology, e.g. "lethal dose of acetaminophen").
142	_LETHAL_GENERIC = re.compile(
143	    r"\b(?:overdose|dose|amount|quantity|how\s+much\s+\w+)\b.{0,45}?"
144	    r"\b(?:to|would|that\s+(?:would|will|could))\s+"
145	    r"(?:cause\s+(?:a\s+|the\s+)?death|be\s+(?:lethal|fatal)|prove\s+fatal)\b",
146	    re.IGNORECASE,
147	)
148	
149	# 2) WEAPONIZATION — Codex brief P2-1: require an ACTION verb (make/build/synthesize/deploy/...), NOT
150	#    a bare subject. "the Chemical Weapons Convention", "nerve-agent treatment guidelines",
151	#    "history of chemical weapons" carry no action verb -> never match.
152	# "bombs?" carries a negative lookahead for benign compounds (Codex diff-gate iter-4: "bomb
153	# calorimeter" / "bath bomb" / "smoke bomb" / "bomb cyclone" are not weapons).
154	_WEAPON_SUBJECT = (
155	    r"(?:bombs?(?!\s+(?:calorimeter|cyclone))|explosives?|explosive\s+device|bioweapon|"
156	    r"biological\s+weapon|chemical\s+weapon|nerve\s+agent|nerve\s+gas|sarin|\bvx\b|mustard\s+gas|"
157	    r"dirty\s+bomb|pipe\s+bomb|\bied\b|grenade|pathogen|anthrax|smallpox|botulinum|ricin|"
158	    r"weaponi[sz]ed\s+pathogen)"
159	)
160	_BENIGN_BOMB = re.compile(r"\b(?:bath|smoke|bond|photo|stink|colou?r)[-\s]?bombs?\b|\bbomb\s+(?:calorimeter|cyclone)\b", re.IGNORECASE)
161	_WEAPONIZATION = re.compile(
162	    # an ACTION verb governing a weapon subject (Codex diff-gate iter-3/4: bare "bomb", "how can I
163	    # make", "instructions/blueprint to build", "engineer anthrax for release" were missed).
164	    # NOTE: "culture/grow" were REMOVED at iter-5 — clinical labs culture pathogens routinely; that
165	    # over-refused legitimate microbiology. Bioweapon engineering is still caught via engineer/
166	    # synthesize/weaponize + the specific agents.
167	    r"\b(?:synthesi[sz]e|synthesi[sz]ing|make|making|build|building|manufactur|produc(?:e|ing)|"
168	    r"construct|creat(?:e|ing)|assembl|deploy|weaponi[sz]|disperse|releas(?:e|ing)|aerosoli[sz]|"
169	    r"engineer(?:ing)?)"
170	    r"\w*\s+(?:a\s+|an\s+|the\s+|some\s+|your\s+own\s+)?"
171	    r".{0,30}?\b" + _WEAPON_SUBJECT
172	    + r"|\bhow\s+(?:to|do\s+i|can\s+i|would\s+i)\s+(?:make|build|synthesi[sz]e|construct|create|"
173	    r"assemble|engineer)\s+(?:a\s+|an\s+)?" + _WEAPON_SUBJECT
174	    + r"|\b(?:recipe|instructions|blueprint|guide|formula|plans?|steps?)\s+(?:for|to\s+(?:make|build|"
175	    r"construct|assemble|engineer))\s+(?:a\s+|an\s+)?" + _WEAPON_SUBJECT
176	    + r"|\b" + _WEAPON_SUBJECT + r"\s+for\s+release\b",
177	    re.IGNORECASE,
178	)
179	
180	# 3) CSAM — unambiguous literal terms + acquisition/generation requests for sexual imagery of minors.
181	#    Codex diff-gate iter-3: "get explicit pictures of underage girls", "where to find underage nudes",
182	#    "generate nude images of a 13 year old" were missed. Any hit -> harmful (no legitimate frame).
183	_MINOR = (
184	    r"(?:minors?|child(?:ren)?|kids?|underage|under-?age|prepubescent|teen(?:age|ager)?s?|"
185	    r"\b(?:a\s+)?\d{1,2}[-\s]?year[-\s]?olds?\b|girls?|boys?)"
186	)
187	# CSAM ACQUISITION / GENERATION (imperative) — NEVER rescued by any frame (Codex diff-gate iter-4:
188	# "show me underage nudes"). Both word orders.
189	_CSAM_ACQUISITION = re.compile(
190	    r"\b(?:get|find|where\s+to\s+(?:find|get|buy)|download|generate|create|make|produce|share|access|"
191	    r"show\s+me|view|stream|trade|sell|buy)\b"
192	    r".{0,30}?\b(?:nudes?|naked|explicit|sexual|porn\w*)\b.{0,20}?\b(?:of\s+)?" + _MINOR
193	    + r"|\b(?:get|find|where\s+to\s+(?:find|get|buy)|download|generate|create|make|produce|share|access|"
194	    r"show\s+me|view|stream|trade|sell|buy)\b"
195	    r".{0,30}?\b" + _MINOR + r"\b.{0,15}?\b(?:nudes?|naked|explicit|sexual|porn\w*)\b"
196	    r"|\b(?:get|find|where\s+to\s+(?:find|get|buy)|download|access|view|trade|sell|buy|show\s+me)\b"
197	    r".{0,20}?\b(?:csam|child\s+porn\w*)\b",
198	    re.IGNORECASE,
199	)
200	# CSAM LITERAL reference — rescuable by a clinical / legal / policy / research SAFE frame anywhere
201	# (Codex diff-gate iter-4/5 over-refusal: "CSAM reporting laws", "clinical treatment of a child sexual
202	# abuse survivor", "legal requirements for reporting sexual abuse of minors", "platform policy for
203	# detecting explicit images of minors" are legitimate). NOTE the bare "[imagery] of [minor]" form
204	# lives HERE (rescuable), NOT in acquisition — only an acquisition VERB is unrescuable (Codex iter-5).
205	_CSAM_LITERAL = re.compile(
206	    r"\b(?:child\s+sexual\s+abuse\s+material|csam|child\s+(?:porn(?:ography)?|sexual\s+abuse)|"
207	    r"sexual\s+abuse\s+of\s+(?:a\s+)?(?:minors?|child(?:ren)?|kids?|underage)|"
208	    r"(?:sexual(?:i[sz]e)?|nude|naked|explicit)\s+\w{0,12}?\s*(?:minor|child|underage|prepubescent)|"
209	    r"(?:minor|child|underage|prepubescent)\s+\w{0,12}?\s*(?:porn|sexual|nude|explicit))\b"
210	    r"|\b(?:nudes?|naked|explicit|sexual|porn\w*)\b.{0,20}?\bof\s+(?:a\s+|an\s+)?" + _MINOR,
211	    re.IGNORECASE,
212	)
213	# Safe frame — "support" is narrowed to victim/survivor support (Codex iter-5: bare "support" let
214	# "how to support child porn creators" bypass).
215	_CSAM_SAFE_GUARD = re.compile(
216	    r"\b(?:report\w*|laws?|legislat\w*|legal|policy|policies|mandatory\s+report\w*|prevent\w*|"
217	    r"treat\w*|therap\w*|clinical|epidemiolog\w*|prevalence|incidence|statistics|survivors?|"
218	    r"\bvictims?\b|(?:victim|survivor)\s+support|support\s+(?:for\s+)?(?:victims?|survivors?)|"
219	    r"hotline|ncmec|investigat\w*|prosecut\w*|takedown|moderation|trust\s+and\s+safety|"
220	    r"detect\w*|hash(?:ing)?|safeguard\w*|protect\w*|awareness|education|recover\w*)\b",
221	    re.IGNORECASE,
222	)
223	
224	# 4) SELF-HARM METHOD-SEEKING — method-seeking, NOT help/awareness. The help/awareness guard below
225	#    vetoes "suicide hotline", "prevention", "signs of", "help a friend who", "support".
226	_SELF_HARM_METHOD = re.compile(
227	    r"\b(?:how\s+(?:do\s+i|to|can\s+i)|best\s+way\s+to|easiest\s+way\s+to|fastest\s+way\s+to|"
228	    r"method[s]?\s+(?:to|of|for)|ways?\s+to|painless\s+way\s+to)\s+"
229	    r"(?:commit\s+suicide|kill\s+myself|end\s+my\s+life|end\s+it\s+all|take\s+my\s+(?:own\s+)?life|"
230	    r"hang\s+myself|overdose\s+(?:on\s+\w+\s+)?(?:to\s+die|and\s+die)|self[-\s]?harm|cut\s+myself)\b"
231	    r"|\bsuicide\s+method[s]?\b|\bhow\s+much\s+\w+\s+(?:to|will)\s+\w*\s*(?:overdose|be\s+lethal|kill\s+me)\b"
232	    # Codex diff-gate iter-4: direct first-person lethal-dose/overdose phrasing.
233	    r"|\b(?:dose|amount|quantity|how\s+much)\b.{0,40}?\b(?:would|to|that\s+would|will)\s+kill\s+me\b"
234	    r"|\bhow\s+much\s+\w+\s+(?:do\s+i\s+need\s+|to\s+|would\s+i\s+need\s+)(?:to\s+)?die\b"
235	    r"|\b(?:lethal|fatal|deadly)\s+(?:dose|amount)\s+(?:of\s+\w+\s+)?(?:for|to\s+kill)\s+(?:myself|me)\b"
236	    r"|\bwhat\s+would\s+kill\s+me\b",
237	    re.IGNORECASE,
238	)
239	# Self-harm RESEARCH / AWARENESS frame — only rescues when it LEADS (see _frame_leads). Codex diff-gate
240	# iter-3: a bare anywhere-match "support"/"resources" let "how to kill myself without my support network
241	# noticing" bypass. First-person method-seeking otherwise ALWAYS refuses-with-redirection (the redirect
242	# IS the help). Legit resource / third-party / awareness queries do not fire _SELF_HARM_METHOD at all.
243	_SELF_HARM_RESEARCH_GUARD = re.compile(
244	    # "research/study" must GOVERN a topic (of/on/into/about) — Codex iter-5: bare "research how to
245	    # kill myself" is an imperative to research a method, not a topic study.
246	    r"\b(?:epidemiolog\w*|prevalence|incidence|statistics|rates?\s+of|"
247	    r"(?:research|study|studies|data|literature)\s+(?:on|of|into|about|regarding)|"
248	    r"news|article|report\w*|history\s+of|prevention\s+(?:program|programme|strateg|campaign)|"
249	    r"public\s+health)\b",
250	    re.IGNORECASE,
251	)
252	
253	# 5) ILLICIT DRUG SYNTHESIS — requires a synthesis/manufacture verb OR a recipe/instructions request.
254	#    "fentanyl overdose deaths", "the opioid epidemic", "methamphetamine addiction treatment" carry no
255	#    synthesis verb -> no match. Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: "recipe for methamphetamine" was missed.
256	_ILLICIT_SYNTHESIS = re.compile(
257	    r"\b(?:synthesi[sz]e|synthesi[sz]ing|cook|cooking|manufactur|produc(?:e|ing)|make|making|"
258	    r"extract(?:ing)?|brew|refin(?:e|ing))\w*\s+(?:a\s+|some\s+|your\s+own\s+|home(?:made)?\s+)?"
259	    r".{0,20}?\b(?:methamphetamine|crystal\s+meth|\bmeth\b|fentanyl|carfentanil|heroin|cocaine|"
260	    r"\bmdma\b|\blsd\b|\bpcp\b|ghb)\b"
261	    r"|\b(?:recipe|instructions|guide|blueprint|formula|protocol)\s+for\s+"
262	    r"(?:making\s+|cooking\s+|synthesi[sz]ing\s+|producing\s+)?"
263	    r"(?:methamphetamine|crystal\s+meth|\bmeth\b|fentanyl|carfentanil|heroin|cocaine|\bmdma\b|"
264	    r"\blsd\b|\bghb\b|\bpcp\b)\b"
265	    r"|\bclandestine\s+(?:drug\s+)?(?:synthesis|lab|manufactur)"
266	    r"|\bsynthesis\s+route\s+(?:for|of)\s+(?:fentanyl|methamphetamine|meth|heroin|mdma|carfentanil|"
267	    r"cocaine|\blsd\b|\bghb\b|\bpcp\b)\b"
268	    r"|\bhow\s+to\s+(?:cook|make|synthesi[sz]e|manufacture)\s+(?:meth|methamphetamine|fentanyl|"
269	    r"crystal\s+meth|heroin|mdma|cocaine)\b",
270	    re.IGNORECASE,
271	)
272	
273	# Prevention / policy / enforcement FRAME for the MAKE/BUILD categories (weaponization, illicit
274	# synthesis). Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1: bare evasion-prone tokens ("enforcement", "surveillance",
275	# "seizure", bare "stop") let "cook meth while avoiding law enforcement" / "synthesize fentanyl
276	# without triggering surveillance" bypass. This now requires genuine POLICY/PREVENTION STRUCTURE
277	# (policy / regulation / treaty / nonproliferation / harm-reduction nouns; "prevent ... from making";
278	# "reduce|disrupt|combat ... production"), AND the classifier requires the frame to LEAD the harm
279	# clause (see _frame_leads) so a TRAILING evasion phrase can never rescue a how-to request.
280	_ACTION_PREVENTION_GUARD = re.compile(
281	    # GOVERNING policy/prevention structures only (Codex diff-gate iter-3: standalone "policy research:"
282	    # / "for policy analysis," prefixes let a how-to-synthesize/build imperative bypass). Each form
283	    # GOVERNS the harm as its object — "prevent ... from making", "reduce|disrupt ... production",
284	    # a policy noun + a governing verb. A loose leading policy noun alone no longer rescues.
285	    # "prevent ... from <making/...>" must govern the harm verb (Codex iter-5: bare "prevent people"
286	    # let "prevent people from stopping me while building a bomb" bypass).
287	    r"\bprevent\w*\s+(?:\w+\s+){0,3}?from\s+(?:making|producing|manufactur|synthesi|building|"
288	    r"developing|acquiring|obtaining|accessing)"
289	    r"|\bprevent\w*\s+(?:the\s+spread\s+of|access\s+to)\b"
290	    r"|\b(?:reduce|disrupt|combat\w*|curb|tackle|ban|banning|restrict\w*|stop|stem|interdict\w*|"
291	    r"intercept)\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:illicit\s+|illegal\s+)?(?:production|traffick\w*|spread|proliferat\w*|"
292	    r"manufactur\w*|supply|distribution|smuggling)\b"
293	    r"|\b(?:public\s+health|policy|regulatory|legislative|law\s+enforcement|harm[-\s]reduction|"
294	    r"nonproliferation|non-?proliferation|treaty|epidemiolog\w*)\s+"
295	    r"(?:program|programme|initiative|intervention|strateg|effort|measure|approach|response|"
296	    r"framework|campaign)s?\b",
297	    re.IGNORECASE,
298	)
299	
300	
301	# Clause separators — a care/prevention frame does NOT govern a harm clause across one of these
302	# (Codex diff-gate iter-4: "clinical management: what dose would kill my roommate" / "policy program:
303	# how to synthesize fentanyl" — a colon-prefixed frame is detached, not governing).
304	_CLAUSE_SEPARATOR = re.compile(r"[:;.?!,–—\n]|\s-\s")
305	
306	
307	def _frame_leads(harm_match: re.Match[str], guard: re.Pattern[str], q: str) -> bool:
308	    """True iff a care/prevention frame GOVERNS the harm clause — it must START BEFORE the harm match
309	    (Codex iter-2: a TRAILING incidental word must not veto) AND there must be NO clause separator
310	    between the frame and the harm (Codex iter-4: a detached "clinical management:" / "research:"
311	    prefix does not govern an imperative harm clause). Only a genuinely governing frame vetoes."""
312	    for g in guard.finditer(q):
313	        if g.start() < harm_match.start():
314	            gap = q[g.end():harm_match.start()]
315	            if not _CLAUSE_SEPARATOR.search(gap):
316	                return True
317	    return False
318	
319	
320	def classify_harm_intent(question: str) -> HarmDecision:
321	    """Classify ``question`` for explicit harm INTENT. Deterministic, fail-open, never raises.
322	
323	    Returns ``harmful=True`` only on an explicit harm-intent / weaponization-action / CSAM /
324	    suicide-method / illicit-synthesis clause. Bare clinical / policy / epidemiology subjects
325	    (no intent clause) return ``harmful=False`` so the legitimate research product is never
326	    over-refused. Each category with a legitimate counterpart (lethal/self-harm → care/help;
327	    weaponization/illicit → policy/prevention) carries its own guard veto — handled INLINE below, NOT
328	    a generic loop, so a guard can never be bypassed. Any internal error returns ``harmful=False``."""
329	    try:
330	        q = (question or "").strip()
331	        if not q:
332	            return HarmDecision(False, "", 0.0, ["empty_question"])
333	
334	        # Self-harm: first-person method-seeking refuses-with-redirection UNLESS a research/awareness
335	        # frame LEADS (a trailing "support network" no longer rescues — Codex diff-gate iter-3).
336	        _m = _SELF_HARM_METHOD.search(q)
337	        if _m and not _frame_leads(_m, _SELF_HARM_RESEARCH_GUARD, q):
338	            return HarmDecision(True, "self_harm_method", 0.9, ["self_harm_method_seeking"])
339	
340	        # Lethal-against-persons: explicit intent AND no care/management frame LEADING the kill clause.
341	        _m = _LETHAL_INTENT.search(q)
342	        if _m and not _frame_leads(_m, _LETHAL_CARE_GUARD, q):
343	            return HarmDecision(
344	                True, "lethal_intent_against_persons", 0.9,
345	                ["lethal_intent_against_persons_explicit_intent"],
346	            )
347	        # Generic un-targeted lethal ("overdose to cause death") — human implied; vetoed by an explicit
348	        # non-person target (science) or a leading care frame.
349	        _m = _LETHAL_GENERIC.search(q)
350	        if _m and not _NONPERSON_TARGET.search(q) and not _frame_leads(_m, _LETHAL_CARE_GUARD, q):
351	            return HarmDecision(
352	                True, "lethal_intent_against_persons", 0.9, ["lethal_generic_cause_death"],
353	            )
354	
355	        # Weaponization: make/build ACTION AND no policy/prevention frame governing it. Benign
356	        # compounds ("bath bomb", "bomb calorimeter") are stripped before matching (Codex iter-4).
357	        _q_w = _BENIGN_BOMB.sub("  ", q)
358	        _m = _WEAPONIZATION.search(_q_w)
359	        if _m and not _frame_leads(_m, _ACTION_PREVENTION_GUARD, _q_w):
360	            return HarmDecision(True, "weaponization", 0.9, ["weaponization_explicit_intent"])
361	
362	        # Illicit synthesis: synthesis verb / recipe AND no policy/prevention frame governing it.
363	        _m = _ILLICIT_SYNTHESIS.search(q)
364	        if _m and not _frame_leads(_m, _ACTION_PREVENTION_GUARD, q):
365	            return HarmDecision(True, "illicit_synthesis", 0.9, ["illicit_synthesis_explicit_intent"])
366	
367	        # CSAM: an ACQUISITION/generation imperative is never rescued; a LITERAL reference is rescued
368	        # only by a clinical/legal/policy/research safe frame (Codex iter-4).
369	        if _CSAM_ACQUISITION.search(q):
370	            return HarmDecision(True, "csam", 0.9, ["csam_acquisition"])
371	        if _CSAM_LITERAL.search(q) and not _CSAM_SAFE_GUARD.search(q):
372	            return HarmDecision(True, "csam", 0.9, ["csam_literal_no_safe_frame"])
373	
374	        return HarmDecision(False, "", 0.0, ["no_explicit_harm_intent"])
375	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail open: a classifier bug must never abort a run.
376	        return HarmDecision(False, "", 0.0, [f"classifier_error_fail_open:{type(exc).__name__}"])
377	
378	
379	# Refuse-with-redirection message (2026 partial-compliance best practice, arXiv 2506.00195) — a
380	# redirection toward legitimate help, NOT a flat "denied". Tunable, not hard-coded at the call site.
381	SAFETY_REFUSAL_REDIRECTION: str = (
382	    "This request appears to seek information that could be used to cause serious harm, so POLARIS "
383	    "will not research it. If you have a legitimate need:\n"
384	    "- Clinical questions (safe dosing, adverse-effect profiles, overdose *management*): consult a "
385	    "licensed clinician, a hospital pharmacy, or your regional poison-control centre.\n"
386	    "- Policy / regulatory / historical questions about controlled materials: rephrase toward the "
387	    "policy, treaty, epidemiology, or clinical-management aspect and resubmit.\n"
388	    "- If you or someone you know is in crisis or considering self-harm: please contact a local "
389	    "emergency line or a suicide-prevention hotline (e.g. 988 in the US/Canada) right now."
390	)

1	"""Phase 1: SCOPE — Query decomposition, perspective discovery, query generation.
2	
3	Decomposes a research query into 6-10 sub-questions, discovers diverse
4	perspectives, and generates targeted search queries per sub-question.
5	
6	This runs BEFORE any search — questions drive evidence collection.
7	
8	Failure modes handled:
9	- F1.1: LLM returns 0 sub-questions → fallback to template queries
10	- F1.2: Degenerate decomposition → diversity gate detects + regenerates
11	- F1.3: Vague query → complexity classifier reduces depth
12	- F1.4: Factual question → simple classification, low depth
13	- F1.5: Unparseable JSON → retry + fallback
14	"""
15	
16	import asyncio
17	import logging
18	import os
19	from typing import Optional
20	
21	from src.polaris_graph.contracts_v3 import (
22	    ScopeOutput,
23	    SearchQuery,
24	    SubQuestion,
25	)
26	
27	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
28	
29	# Maximum LLM attempts before fallback (F1.1, F1.5)
30	_MAX_SCOPE_RETRIES = 2
31	
32	# Diversity gate threshold — cosine similarity above this = too similar (F1.2)
33	_DIVERSITY_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = float(
34	    os.getenv("PG_V3_SCOPE_DIVERSITY_THRESHOLD", "0.85")
35	)
36	
37	# Minimum distinct analytical focuses required (F1.2)
38	_MIN_DISTINCT_FOCUSES = 3
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Decomposition system prompt
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	
45	_SCOPE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a research strategist. Given a research topic, decompose it into 6-10 sub-questions that a knowledgeable reader would want answered.
46	
47	Requirements:
48	- Questions should flow logically: context → mechanisms → effectiveness → comparison → limitations → future
49	- Each question must target a DIFFERENT aspect of the topic — no paraphrasing
50	- Assign each question an analytical_focus from: aggregate, compare, explain, tabulate, challenge
51	- Assign depth: 'deep' for core questions (2-3), 'moderate' for supporting (3-4), 'brief' for peripheral (1-2)
52	- The set of questions should cover: what, how, how well, compared to what, and what's missing
53	- At least 3 different analytical_focus values must be represented
54	- Generate a narrative_flow description explaining how questions build on each other
55	
56	Also provide:
57	- 5-8 diverse perspectives (e.g., Scientific, Engineering, Economic, Regulatory, Environmental)
58	- 3-5 search queries per sub-question, each tagged with sub_question_id, perspective, and source_preference (web|academic|both)
59	- A complexity classification: 'simple' (factual/narrow), 'moderate' (multi-faceted), 'complex' (broad/deep)
60	- An estimated_depth (target evidence count): simple=30-50, moderate=100-200, complex=200-500""".strip()
61	
62	
63	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	# Perspective templates (from STORM, used in fallback)
65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	
67	_DEFAULT_PERSPECTIVES = [
68	    "Scientific",
69	    "Engineering",
70	    "Environmental",
71	    "Economic",
72	    "Regulatory",
73	    "Public_Health",
74	    "Industry",
75	]

79	# Public API

81	
82	async def run_scope(
83	    client,
84	    query: str,
85	    application: str = "",
86	    region: str = "",
87	    research_brief: str = "",
88	    ltm_priors: Optional[list[str]] = None,
89	) -> ScopeOutput:
90	    """Phase 1: Decompose query into sub-questions and search queries.
91	
92	    Args:
93	        client: OpenRouterClient (or mock).
94	        query: The research query.
95	        application: Domain context (e.g., "water_treatment").
96	        region: Geographic context (e.g., "global").
97	        research_brief: Optional domain context from Campaign Control Center.
98	        ltm_priors: Optional list of prior findings from LTM memory.
99	
100	    Returns:
101	        ScopeOutput with sub-questions, perspectives, and search queries.
102	        Always returns a valid ScopeOutput — falls back to templates on failure.
103	    """
104	    # Build the decomposition prompt
105	    context_parts = [f"Research topic: {query}"]
106	    if application:
107	        context_parts.append(f"Application domain: {application}")
108	    if region:
109	        context_parts.append(f"Geographic scope: {region}")
110	    if research_brief:
111	        context_parts.append(f"Research brief: {research_brief[:500]}")
112	    if ltm_priors:
113	        prior_text = "\n".join(f"- {p}" for p in ltm_priors[:5])
114	        context_parts.append(f"Prior findings from previous research:\n{prior_text}")
115	
116	    prompt = "\n".join(context_parts)
117	
118	    # Attempt LLM decomposition with retries (F1.1, F1.5)
119	    scope_output = None
120	    for attempt in range(_MAX_SCOPE_RETRIES):
121	        try:
122	            scope_output = await client.generate_structured(
123	                prompt=prompt,
124	                schema=ScopeOutput,
125	                system=_SCOPE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
126	                max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_SCOPE_MAX_TOKENS", "8192")),
127	                timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_SCOPE_TIMEOUT", "120")),
128	            )
129	            if scope_output and len(scope_output.sub_questions) >= 3:
130	                break
131	            logger.warning(
132	                "[v3 scope] Attempt %d: got %d sub-questions (need >= 3), retrying",
133	                attempt + 1,
134	                len(scope_output.sub_questions) if scope_output else 0,
135	            )
136	            scope_output = None
137	        except Exception as exc:
138	            logger.warning(
139	                "[v3 scope] Attempt %d failed: %s",
140	                attempt + 1,
141	                str(exc)[:200],
142	            )
143	            scope_output = None
144	
145	    # F1.2: Check diversity if LLM succeeded
146	    if scope_output is not None:
147	        is_diverse = await _check_question_diversity(scope_output.sub_questions)
148	        if not is_diverse:
149	            logger.warning(
150	                "[v3 scope] Sub-questions failed diversity gate, regenerating with diversity instruction"
151	            )
152	            # One more attempt with explicit diversity instruction
153	            try:
154	                scope_output = await client.generate_structured(
155	                    prompt=prompt + "\n\nCRITICAL: Each sub-question MUST address a DIFFERENT aspect. "
156	                    "Do NOT paraphrase the same question. Cover: mechanisms, conditions, "
157	                    "effectiveness, comparison, limitations, economics.",
158	                    schema=ScopeOutput,
159	                    system=_SCOPE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
160	                    max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_SCOPE_MAX_TOKENS", "8192")),
161	                    timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_SCOPE_TIMEOUT", "120")),
162	                )
163	                if not scope_output or len(scope_output.sub_questions) < 3:
164	                    scope_output = None
165	            except Exception:
166	                scope_output = None
167	
168	    # Fallback: template-based decomposition (F1.1, F1.5)
169	    if scope_output is None:
170	        logger.warning("[v3 scope] All LLM attempts failed, using template fallback")
171	        scope_output = _fallback_scope(query, application, region)
172	
173	    # If LLM produced sub-questions but no search queries, generate them
174	    if scope_output and len(scope_output.search_queries) == 0 and len(scope_output.sub_questions) > 0:
175	        logger.info("[v3 scope] LLM omitted search_queries, generating from sub-questions")
176	        generated_queries = []
177	        for sq in scope_output.sub_questions:
178	            # 2 queries per sub-question: one web, one academic
179	            generated_queries.append(SearchQuery(
180	                query=f"{query} {sq.question}",
181	                sub_question_id=sq.id,
182	                perspective=scope_output.perspectives[0] if scope_output.perspectives else "Scientific",
183	                source_preference="both",
184	            ))
185	            generated_queries.append(SearchQuery(
186	                query=sq.question,
187	                sub_question_id=sq.id,
188	                perspective=scope_output.perspectives[1] if len(scope_output.perspectives) > 1 else "Scientific",
189	                source_preference="academic",
190	            ))
191	        scope_output = ScopeOutput(
192	            sub_questions=scope_output.sub_questions,
193	            perspectives=scope_output.perspectives,
194	            search_queries=generated_queries,
195	            complexity=scope_output.complexity,
196	            estimated_depth=scope_output.estimated_depth,
197	        )
198	
199	    logger.info(
200	        "[v3 scope] Decomposed '%s' into %d sub-questions, %d perspectives, %d queries (complexity=%s)",
201	        query[:60],
202	        len(scope_output.sub_questions),
203	        len(scope_output.perspectives),
204	        len(scope_output.search_queries),
205	        scope_output.complexity,
206	    )
207	
208	    return scope_output
209

211	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	# Diversity gate (F1.2)
213	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	async def _check_question_diversity(
216	    questions: list[SubQuestion],
217	) -> bool:
218	    """Check that sub-questions are genuinely diverse, not paraphrases.
219	
220	    Uses two signals:
221	    1. Analytical focus variety (must have >= 3 distinct focuses)
222	    2. Word overlap (Jaccard similarity — cheap, no embedding needed)
223	
224	    Returns True if questions are sufficiently diverse.
225	    """
226	    if len(questions) < 3:
227	        return False
228	
229	    # Signal 1: Analytical focus variety
230	    focuses = {q.analytical_focus for q in questions}
231	    if len(focuses) < _MIN_DISTINCT_FOCUSES:
232	        logger.debug(
233	            "[v3 scope] Diversity fail: only %d distinct focuses (%s)",
234	            len(focuses), focuses,
235	        )
236	        return False
237	
238	    # Signal 2: Jaccard word overlap between all pairs
239	    # If >50% of pairs have Jaccard > 0.6, questions are too similar
240	    high_overlap_count = 0
241	    total_pairs = 0
242	
243	    for i in range(len(questions)):
244	        words_i = set(questions[i].question.lower().split())
245	        for j in range(i + 1, len(questions)):
246	            words_j = set(questions[j].question.lower().split())
247	            intersection = words_i & words_j
248	            union = words_i | words_j
249	            jaccard = len(intersection) / max(len(union), 1)
250	            total_pairs += 1
251	            if jaccard > 0.6:
252	                high_overlap_count += 1
253	
254	    if total_pairs > 0 and high_overlap_count / total_pairs > 0.5:
255	        logger.debug(
256	            "[v3 scope] Diversity fail: %d/%d pairs have Jaccard > 0.6",
257	            high_overlap_count, total_pairs,
258	        )
259	        return False
260	
261	    return True
262	
263	
264	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
265	# Template fallback (F1.1, F1.5)
266	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
267	
268	def _fallback_scope(
269	    query: str,
270	    application: str = "",
271	    region: str = "",
272	) -> ScopeOutput:
273	    """Generate a template-based ScopeOutput when LLM fails.
274	
275	    Produces 6 standard sub-questions covering the canonical research
276	    structure: what, how, how well, compared to what, limitations, future.
277	    """
278	    topic = query.strip()
279	    app_suffix = f" in {application}" if application else ""
280	    region_suffix = f" ({region})" if region else ""
281	
282	    sub_questions = [
283	        SubQuestion(
284	            id="sq_01",
285	            question=f"What are the fundamental mechanisms of {topic}{app_suffix}?",
286	            analytical_focus="explain",
287	            expected_depth="deep",
288	        ),
289	        SubQuestion(
290	            id="sq_02",
291	            question=f"What conditions and parameters affect the performance of {topic}?",
292	            analytical_focus="compare",
293	            expected_depth="deep",
294	        ),
295	        SubQuestion(
296	            id="sq_03",
297	            question=f"What quantitative effectiveness has been reported for {topic}{region_suffix}?",
298	            analytical_focus="aggregate",
299	            expected_depth="deep",
300	        ),
301	        SubQuestion(
302	            id="sq_04",
303	            question=f"How does {topic} compare to alternative approaches?",
304	            analytical_focus="tabulate",
305	            expected_depth="moderate",
306	        ),
307	        SubQuestion(
308	            id="sq_05",
309	            question=f"What are the known limitations and knowledge gaps in {topic}?",
310	            analytical_focus="challenge",
311	            expected_depth="moderate",
312	        ),
313	        SubQuestion(
314	            id="sq_06",
315	            question=f"What are the cost-effectiveness and practical considerations for {topic}?",
316	            analytical_focus="compare",
317	            expected_depth="brief",
318	        ),
319	    ]
320	
321	    perspectives = _DEFAULT_PERSPECTIVES[:5]
322	
323	    # Generate search queries using the existing fallback function
324	    try:
325	        from src.polaris_graph.agents.planner import _fallback_queries
326	        raw_queries = _fallback_queries(query, application, region)
327	    except Exception:
328	        # Absolute last resort: simple template queries
329	        raw_queries = [
330	            f"{topic} mechanisms",
331	            f"{topic} effectiveness results",
332	            f"{topic} comparison alternatives",
333	            f"{topic} limitations challenges",
334	            f"{topic} cost analysis",
335	            f"{topic} recent research 2024 2025",
336	        ]
337	
338	    # Distribute queries across sub-questions
339	    search_queries = []
340	    for i, raw_q in enumerate(raw_queries[:18]):  # Cap at 3 per sub-question
341	        sq_idx = i % len(sub_questions)
342	        sq_id = sub_questions[sq_idx].id
343	        perspective = perspectives[i % len(perspectives)]
344	        search_queries.append(SearchQuery(
345	            query=raw_q,
346	            sub_question_id=sq_id,
347	            perspective=perspective,
348	            source_preference="both" if i % 3 != 2 else "academic",
349	        ))
350	
351	    return ScopeOutput(
352	        sub_questions=sub_questions,
353	        perspectives=perspectives,
354	        search_queries=search_queries,
355	        complexity="moderate",
356	        estimated_depth=200,
357	    )

1	"""
2	Phase 2b — Scope gate + pre-registered protocol.json at T+0.
3	
4	Part of HONEST-REBUILD Phase 2 (plan:
5	C:/Users/msn/.claude/plans/lovely-finding-firefly.md).
6	
7	THE PROBLEM THIS SOLVES
8	-----------------------
9	PG_LB_SA_02_CONTENT_AUDIT.md Section F-02 documented that the pre-rebuild
10	pipeline allowed scope drift: the final report used inclusion criteria
11	that were not pre-registered, the user could not tell when the generator
12	silently broadened or narrowed the query, and PRISMA-style methods
13	sections were reconstructed post-hoc rather than locked before retrieval.
14	
15	Pre-registration is the standard mitigation in systematic review
16	methodology (PROSPERO, OSF Registries, NIH protocol registration):
17	you state the question + inclusion/exclusion + expected tier distribution
18	BEFORE you see the evidence. Any later deviation is documented, not
19	hidden.
20	
21	WHAT THIS NODE DOES
22	-------------------
23	1. Takes the raw user query + domain hint.
24	2. Loads the matching scope template from `config/scope_templates/`
25	   (clinical / policy / tech / due_diligence) — see Phase 2c.
26	3. Structures the query into a `ProtocolDocument`:
27	   - Research question (verbatim user input)
28	   - PICO / population / intervention / comparator / outcome (when clinical)
29	   - Inclusion criteria (derived from template + user overrides)
30	   - Exclusion criteria (derived from template + user overrides)
31	   - Expected tier mix (derived from template)
32	   - Acceptable evidence horizons (date range, geography, languages)
33	4. Writes `{run_dir}/protocol.json` and stamps a SHA-256 over it.
34	5. Returns the protocol path + hash so downstream nodes can attach the
35	   hash to every artifact for tamper-evidence.
36	
37	No LLM is called. This node is deterministic and rule-based so the
38	protocol cannot be hallucinated. If the template-driven derivation is
39	insufficient, the node logs a warning and marks the protocol as
40	`needs_user_review: true` — a human should confirm before retrieval.
41	"""

43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import hashlib
46	import json
47	import logging
48	import os
49	import re
50	import time

53	from typing import Any, Optional

97	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

102	@dataclass
103	class TierExpectation:
104	    """Expected lower / upper bounds for a single tier in the final corpus.
105	
106	    The pipeline does NOT fail if actual distribution differs — but the
107	    corpus-approval gate (Phase 2g) shows the delta to the user so they
108	    can reject a corpus dominated by commentary when the protocol asked
109	    for ≥40% primary studies.
110	    """
111	
112	    tier: str          # "T1" .. "T7" or "UNKNOWN"
113	    min_fraction: float  # 0.0-1.0
114	    max_fraction: float  # 0.0-1.0
115	    rationale: str       # human-readable reason ("RCT-heavy for efficacy")
116	
117	
118	@dataclass
119	class InclusionExclusionCriteria:
120	    """PRISMA-style inclusion / exclusion criteria."""
121	
122	    inclusion: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
123	    exclusion: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
124	
125	    def normalize(self) -> "InclusionExclusionCriteria":
126	        """Deduplicate and trim."""
127	        inc = sorted({i.strip() for i in self.inclusion if i.strip()})
128	        exc = sorted({e.strip() for e in self.exclusion if e.strip()})
129	        return InclusionExclusionCriteria(inclusion=inc, exclusion=exc)
130	
131	
132	@dataclass
133	class ProtocolDocument:
134	    """Pre-registered protocol. Immutable once written to protocol.json."""
135	
136	    # Identity
137	    run_id: str
138	    created_at_unix: float
139	    created_at_iso: str
140	
141	    # Core question
142	    research_question: str
143	    domain: str               # clinical / policy / tech / due_diligence
144	
145	    # Optional PICO framing (clinical domain only; may be empty for others).
146	    population: Optional[str] = None
147	    intervention: Optional[str] = None
148	    comparator: Optional[str] = None
149	    outcome: Optional[str] = None
150	
151	    # Inclusion / exclusion
152	    criteria: InclusionExclusionCriteria = field(
153	        default_factory=InclusionExclusionCriteria
154	    )
155	
156	    # Expected tier mix (from template)
157	    expected_tier_distribution: list[TierExpectation] = field(
158	        default_factory=list
159	    )
160	
161	    # Evidence horizons

163	    geography: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
164	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: ["en"])
165	
166	    # I-deepfix-001 B10(a) (#1352): structured HARD user constraints extracted
167	    # from the NL research question at intake (date window / language / journal-
168	    # only-dormant). to_json_dict serializes this so the wave-2 enforcement layers
169	    # (selection demote, rule_blockers) and the audit trail can read it. Empty
170	    # dict when the extractor is OFF or found nothing => byte-identical legacy
171	    # protocol shape for the populated fields.
172	    user_constraints: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
173	
174	    # I-scope-001: structured per-question SCOPE intent (source-type / jurisdiction facets +
175	    # include/prefer/exclude op + weight/hard strictness + named include/exclude). Empty dict
176	    # when the extractor is OFF or found nothing => the enforcer builds empty maps => byte-
177	    # identical widest+deepest run. to_json_dict() serializes this via asdict() as an ADDITIVE
178	    # key (`scope_constraints: {}` on the no-constraint path — the ONE inert protocol.json
179	    # difference vs today). Read by the scope enforcer only; no abort/approval/release gate.
180	    scope_constraints: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
181

193	    # needs_user_review. scope_decision is one of:
194	    #   "proceed" — pipeline should continue

196	    #               uncertain and a human should verify assumptions

214	class ScopeGateResult:
215	    """Return value of run_scope_gate()."""
216	
217	    protocol: ProtocolDocument
218	    protocol_path: Path
219	    protocol_sha256: str

222	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
223	# Template loader
224	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
225	
226	
227	def load_scope_template(domain: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
228	    """Load `config/scope_templates/{domain}.yaml` as a dict.
229	
230	    Raises RuntimeError if the file doesn't exist or yaml isn't installed.
231	    """
232	    if yaml is None:
233	        raise RuntimeError(
234	            "PyYAML is required for scope_gate. Install via "
235	            "`pip install pyyaml`."
236	        )
237	    if domain not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS:
238	        raise ValueError(
239	            f"domain={domain!r} not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS. "
240	            f"Options: {sorted(SUPPORTED_DOMAINS)}"
241	        )
242	    path = _SCOPE_TEMPLATES_DIR / f"{domain}.yaml"
243	    if not path.exists():
244	        raise RuntimeError(
245	            f"Scope template {path} does not exist. Phase 2c is responsible "
246	            f"for creating this file; run the rebuild Phase 2c step first."
247	        )
248	    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
249	        data = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}
250	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
251	        raise RuntimeError(
252	            f"Scope template {path} did not parse to a dict (got "
253	            f"{type(data).__name__}). Check the YAML syntax."
254	        )
255	    return data
256	
257	
258	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
259	# Lightweight PICO extractor (clinical domain only)
260	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
261	
262	# These are regex heuristics, not an LLM. The point of the scope gate
263	# is that the protocol is deterministic — an LLM-extracted PICO would be
264	# hallucinable. The user can override any field via explicit kwargs.
265	
266	_DRUG_NAME_RE = re.compile(
267	    r"\b("
268	    # GLP-1 analogues (canonical test domain)
269	    r"semaglutide|tirzepatide|liraglutide|dulaglutide|exenatide|lixisenatide|"
270	    r"retatrutide|cagrilintide|orforglipron|"
271	    r"metformin|empagliflozin|dapagliflozin|canagliflozin|sitagliptin|"
272	    # Oncology examples for generality
273	    r"pembrolizumab|nivolumab|trastuzumab|rituximab|"
274	    r"imatinib|osimertinib"
275	    r")\b",
276	    re.IGNORECASE,
277	)
278	# NOTE: `_DRUG_NAME_RE` (the literal canonical-drug allowlist above) is
279	# DELIBERATELY left unchanged. Three modules import it for subject extraction
280	# in conflict/contradiction detection and completeness token substitution
281	# (completeness_checker, contradiction_detector, qualitative_conflict_detector)
282	# — that is a SEPARATE concern from the scope false-abort fixed by F13. The
283	# A3-F13 fix adds a config-driven *recognizer* (`_intervention_present`) used by
284	# the scope gate's PICO heuristic so an OFF-LIST drug no longer false-aborts;
285	# the literal regex stays as the canonical-name fast path + the consumers' API.
286	
287	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
288	# A3 fix F13 (GH I-arch-002) — config-driven intervention recognizer.
289	#
290	# The scope gate previously recognised a clinical *intervention* ONLY via the
291	# ~25-entry hard-coded `_DRUG_NAME_RE` allowlist above. A clinical question about
292	# any drug NOT in that list whose population also could not be extracted read
293	# BOTH PICO anchors as None and was hard-rejected (abort_scope_rejected /
294	# clinical_pico_unscoped) — a false abort on a perfectly well-scoped question.
295	#
296	# LAW VI (zero hard-coding): the recognizer below is fully config-driven from
297	# `config/clinical_safety/intervention_recognition.yaml` — WHO/USAN INN class
298	# stems (generative drug recognition) + a seed list of stemless legacy names.
299	# No closed per-drug allowlist gates the decision. The gate stays STRICT: a
300	# genuinely contentless clinical question (no stem, no known name, no
301	# population) still rejects. This touches NO faithfulness gate.
302	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
303	
304	_INTERVENTION_RECOGNITION_CONFIG = (
305	    _REPO_ROOT / "config" / "clinical_safety" / "intervention_recognition.yaml"
306	)
307	
308	
309	@dataclass(frozen=True)
310	class _InterventionRecognizer:
311	    """Compiled config-driven recognizer for a clinical intervention name."""
312	
313	    inn_stem_re: "_MinLenStemPattern"
314	    known_names_re: re.Pattern[str]
315	    exclude_words: frozenset[str]
316	    # I-arch-011 FIX-P0-B (GENERAL, non-drug interventions): a DEVICE (deep brain
317	    # stimulator, pacemaker, implant, stent) or a PROCEDURE (ablation,
318	    # transplantation, -ectomy) must be recognised as an intervention too, not
319	    # just a drug — otherwise the completeness gate marks device/procedure-safety
320	    # slots NON-applicable in EVERY non-pharma domain. Optional (absent config
321	    # sections -> None -> no match; backward-compatible). Domain-general,
322	    # config-driven (LAW VI), NOT a per-disease/per-device allowlist.
323	    procedure_stem_re: Optional["_MinLenStemPattern"] = None
324	    device_term_re: Optional[re.Pattern[str]] = None
325	
326	    def _known_search(self, text: str) -> Optional[re.Match[str]]:
327	        """Earliest known-name match whose token is not in the denylist."""
328	        for m in self.known_names_re.finditer(text):
329	            if m.group(0).lower() not in self.exclude_words:
330	                return m
331	        return None
332	
333	    def find(self, text: str) -> Optional[str]:
334	        """Return the first recognised intervention token (lowercased) or None.
335	
336	        The token that appears EARLIEST in the text wins across ALL recognisers
337	        (drug INN-stem, legacy drug name, device term, procedure stem); on an
338	        exact-position tie the known drug-name is preferred (it is appended first
339	        below). Any single intervention anchor is sufficient for the scope
340	        decision, so the tie-break only affects which token is *reported*, never
341	        whether the gate proceeds. Tokens in `exclude_words` (common-English
342	        collisions, e.g. accept/except for the `-cept` stem) are never recognised.
343	        """
344	        if not text:
345	            return None
346	        candidates: list[re.Match[str]] = []
347	        known = self._known_search(text)
348	        if known is not None:
349	            candidates.append(known)
350	        stem = self.inn_stem_re.search(text)  # already denylist-filtered
351	        if stem is not None:
352	            candidates.append(stem)
353	        if self.device_term_re is not None:
354	            dev = self.device_term_re.search(text)
355	            if dev is not None:
356	                candidates.append(dev)
357	        if self.procedure_stem_re is not None:
358	            proc = self.procedure_stem_re.search(text)
359	            if proc is not None:
360	                candidates.append(proc)
361	        if not candidates:
362	            return None
363	        # min() keeps the FIRST candidate on an exact-position tie -> known drug
364	        # name (appended first) wins, preserving the prior reporting preference.
365	        chosen = min(candidates, key=lambda m: m.start())
366	        return chosen.group(0).lower()
367	
368	
369	def _build_intervention_recognizer(
370	    config_path: Path,
371	) -> _InterventionRecognizer:
372	    """Compile the INN-stem + known-name recognizer from YAML (fail-loud).
373	
374	    Raises RuntimeError if PyYAML is unavailable, the config file is missing,
375	    or a required section is absent/empty. No silent fallback (LAW II).
376	    """
377	    if yaml is None:
378	        raise RuntimeError(
379	            "PyYAML is required for intervention recognition. Install via "
380	            "`pip install pyyaml`."
381	        )
382	    if not config_path.exists():
383	        raise RuntimeError(
384	            f"Intervention-recognition config {config_path} does not exist. "
385	            f"A3 fix F13 requires this file (LAW VI: no hard-coded allowlist)."
386	        )
387	    with config_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
388	        data = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}
389	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
390	        raise RuntimeError(
391	            f"Intervention-recognition config {config_path} did not parse to a "
392	            f"dict (got {type(data).__name__}). Check the YAML syntax."
393	        )
394	
395	    inn = data.get("inn_stems") or {}
396	    if not isinstance(inn, dict):
397	        raise RuntimeError(
398	            f"{config_path}: 'inn_stems' must be a mapping, got "
399	            f"{type(inn).__name__}."
400	        )
401	    stems = inn.get("stems") or []
402	    stems = [str(s).strip().lower() for s in stems if str(s).strip()]
403	    if not stems:
404	        raise RuntimeError(
405	            f"{config_path}: 'inn_stems.stems' must be a non-empty list."
406	        )
407	    try:
408	        min_prefix_len = int(inn.get("min_prefix_len", 2))
409	        min_token_len = int(inn.get("min_token_len", 6))
410	    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
411	        raise RuntimeError(
412	            f"{config_path}: min_prefix_len/min_token_len must be integers: {exc}"
413	        ) from exc
414	    if min_prefix_len < 1 or min_token_len < 1:
415	        raise RuntimeError(
416	            f"{config_path}: min_prefix_len and min_token_len must be >= 1."
417	        )
418	
419	    # Precision guard: every stem must be preceded, within the SAME word, by at
420	    # least `min_prefix_len` alphabetic characters, and the whole token must be
421	    # at least `min_token_len` chars long. This stops common English words that
422	    # merely end in a stem-looking substring (machine/April/pine) from matching.
423	    # Longest-stem-first so "-tinib" wins over "-nib", "-navir" over "-vir".
424	    stems_sorted = sorted(set(stems), key=len, reverse=True)
425	    stem_alt = "|".join(re.escape(s) for s in stems_sorted)
426	    # \b[a-z]{>=min_prefix_len}(?:stem)\b — then enforce min_token_len in code.
427	    inn_stem_re = re.compile(
428	        rf"\b([a-z]{{{min_prefix_len},}}(?:{stem_alt}))\b",
429	        re.IGNORECASE,
430	    )
431	
432	    known = data.get("known_names") or []
433	    known = [str(n).strip().lower() for n in known if str(n).strip()]
434	    if not known:
435	        raise RuntimeError(
436	            f"{config_path}: 'known_names' must be a non-empty seed list."
437	        )
438	    known_sorted = sorted(set(known), key=len, reverse=True)
439	    known_alt = "|".join(re.escape(n) for n in known_sorted)
440	    known_names_re = re.compile(rf"\b({known_alt})\b", re.IGNORECASE)
441	
442	    # exclude_words is OPTIONAL (a denylist of common-English collisions). An
443	    # absent/empty section is valid — the anchoring + min-length guards still
444	    # apply; the denylist only adds precision for residual collisions like the
445	    # `-cept` stem vs accept/except/concept/intercept.
446	    exclude_raw = data.get("exclude_words") or []
447	    if not isinstance(exclude_raw, list):
448	        raise RuntimeError(
449	            f"{config_path}: 'exclude_words' must be a list when present, got "
450	            f"{type(exclude_raw).__name__}."
451	        )
452	    exclude_words = frozenset(
453	        str(w).strip().lower() for w in exclude_raw if str(w).strip()
454	    )
455	
456	    # I-arch-011 FIX-P0-B (GENERAL device/procedure recognition). Both sections
457	    # are OPTIONAL — absent => the recogniser stays drug-only (backward-compatible).
458	    # `procedure_stems` mirrors `inn_stems` (morphological suffixes that coin
459	    # procedure names, e.g. -ectomy/-ostomy/-oscopy); `device_procedure_terms` is
460	    # a seed list of stemless device/procedure NOUNS/phrases (deep brain
461	    # stimulation, pacemaker, ablation, dialysis, ...). Domain-general, LAW VI.
462	    procedure_stem_re: Optional[_MinLenStemPattern] = None
463	    proc = data.get("procedure_stems")
464	    if isinstance(proc, dict):
465	        p_stems = [str(s).strip().lower() for s in (proc.get("stems") or []) if str(s).strip()]
466	        if p_stems:
467	            try:
468	                p_min_prefix = int(proc.get("min_prefix_len", 3))
469	                p_min_token = int(proc.get("min_token_len", 7))
470	            except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
471	                raise RuntimeError(
472	                    f"{config_path}: procedure_stems min_prefix_len/min_token_len "
473	                    f"must be integers: {exc}"
474	                ) from exc
475	            if p_min_prefix < 1 or p_min_token < 1:
476	                raise RuntimeError(
477	                    f"{config_path}: procedure_stems min_prefix_len/min_token_len "
478	                    f"must be >= 1."
479	                )
480	            p_sorted = sorted(set(p_stems), key=len, reverse=True)
481	            p_alt = "|".join(re.escape(s) for s in p_sorted)
482	            p_re = re.compile(
483	                rf"\b([a-z]{{{p_min_prefix},}}(?:{p_alt}))\b", re.IGNORECASE
484	            )
485	            procedure_stem_re = _MinLenStemPattern(p_re, p_min_token, exclude_words)
486	
487	    device_term_re: Optional[re.Pattern[str]] = None
488	    dev_terms = data.get("device_procedure_terms") or []
489	    if not isinstance(dev_terms, list):
490	        raise RuntimeError(
491	            f"{config_path}: 'device_procedure_terms' must be a list when "
492	            f"present, got {type(dev_terms).__name__}."
493	        )
494	    dev_terms = [str(t).strip().lower() for t in dev_terms if str(t).strip()]
495	    if dev_terms:
496	        # Longest phrase first so "deep brain stimulation" wins over "stimulation".
497	        dev_sorted = sorted(set(dev_terms), key=len, reverse=True)
498	        dev_alt = "|".join(re.escape(t) for t in dev_sorted)
499	        device_term_re = re.compile(rf"\b({dev_alt})\b", re.IGNORECASE)
500	
501	    return _InterventionRecognizer(
502	        inn_stem_re=_MinLenStemPattern(inn_stem_re, min_token_len, exclude_words),
503	        known_names_re=known_names_re,
504	        exclude_words=exclude_words,
505	        procedure_stem_re=procedure_stem_re,
506	        device_term_re=device_term_re,
507	    )

510	class _MinLenStemPattern:
511	    """Wrap an INN-stem regex to enforce min token length + a denylist.
512	
513	    `re.Pattern` cannot express "the captured token must be >= N chars and not
514	    in this denylist" cleanly alongside a stem alternation, so we post-filter
515	    matches. The denylist removes residual common-English collisions that the
516	    stem anchoring + min-length cannot separate (e.g. accept/except/concept/
517	    intercept for the `-cept` fusion-protein stem). Exposes the subset of the
518	    `re.Pattern` API the recognizer uses (`search`).
519	    """
520	
521	    def __init__(
522	        self,
523	        pattern: re.Pattern[str],
524	        min_token_len: int,
525	        exclude_words: frozenset[str],
526	    ) -> None:
527	        self._pattern = pattern
528	        self._min_token_len = min_token_len
529	        self._exclude_words = exclude_words
530	
531	    def search(self, text: str) -> Optional[re.Match[str]]:
532	        for m in self._pattern.finditer(text):
533	            token = m.group(0)
534	            if len(token) < self._min_token_len:
535	                continue
536	            if token.lower() in self._exclude_words:
537	                continue
538	            return m
539	        return None

562	def _intervention_present(query: str) -> Optional[str]:
563	    """Return a recognised intervention token (lowercased) from `query` or None.
564	
565	    Two layers, both config-driven (LAW VI):
566	      1. The canonical `_DRUG_NAME_RE` literal fast path (exact known names) —
567	         preserves byte-for-byte the prior matches/case for those drugs.
568	      2. The config-driven INN-stem + known-name recognizer (off-list drugs).
569	
570	    Returns None when no intervention is recognised, so a genuinely unscoped
571	    clinical question still reads intervention=None and the gate stays strict.
572	    """
573	    q = (query or "").strip()
574	    if not q:
575	        return None
576	    canonical = _DRUG_NAME_RE.search(q)
577	    if canonical:
578	        return canonical.group(1).lower()
579	    return _get_intervention_recognizer().find(q)

582	_POPULATION_MARKERS_RE = re.compile(
583	    r"\b("
584	    r"adults?|children|elderly|pediatric|geriatric|adolescents?|"
585	    r"pregnant|postmenopausal|premenopausal|"
586	    r"type\s*1\s*diabetes|type\s*2\s*diabetes|t1dm|t2dm|"
587	    r"obesity|overweight|obese|"
588	    r"chronic kidney disease|ckd|"
589	    r"heart failure|hf|"
590	    r"NAFLD|NASH|MASH|MASLD"
591	    r")\b",
592	    re.IGNORECASE,
593	)

596	def extract_pico_heuristic(query: str) -> dict[str, Optional[str]]:
597	    """Extract a provisional PICO framing from the query string.
598	
599	    This is heuristic only. The caller SHOULD override these when better
600	    information is available. We return everything lowercased for later
601	    case-insensitive comparison, and we return None when no marker is
602	    found (rather than making up a placeholder).
603	    """
604	    q = query.strip()
605	    result: dict[str, Optional[str]] = {
606	        "population": None,
607	        "intervention": None,
608	        "comparator": None,
609	        "outcome": None,
610	    }
611	    pop = _POPULATION_MARKERS_RE.search(q)
612	    if pop:
613	        result["population"] = pop.group(1).lower()
614	    # F13: config-driven recognizer (canonical fast path + INN stems + known
615	    # names) instead of the bare ~25-drug `_DRUG_NAME_RE` allowlist, so an
616	    # off-list drug is recognised and no longer false-aborts the scope gate.
617	    intervention = _intervention_present(q)
618	    if intervention:
619	        result["intervention"] = intervention
620	
621	    # Outcome detection is weak; look for standard trial outcomes
622	    q_l = q.lower()
623	    for outcome_marker in (
624	        "weight loss", "hba1c", "mortality", "major adverse cardiac events",
625	        "mace", "blood pressure", "ldl", "glycemic control",
626	        "disease-free survival", "overall survival", "quality of life",
627	    ):
628	        if outcome_marker in q_l:
629	            result["outcome"] = outcome_marker
630	            break
631	    return result

634	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
635	# I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985, brief §2.2): field-agnostic frame extractor.
636	#
637	# ADDITIVE. The clinical `extract_pico_heuristic` + `_DRUG_NAME_RE` above are
638	# UNCHANGED (off-path + the existing importers in completeness_checker.py and
639	# contradiction_detector.py continue to use them). This heuristic does NOT use
640	# the clinical drug/population regex; it produces a lightweight on-path frame
641	# for ANY field by content-word extraction, with NO clinical literal as a
642	# control value. It is a deterministic fallback only — the field-agnostic
643	# planner (planning.research_planner) is the primary on-mode frame source; this
644	# heuristic seeds entities/metrics/comparators when no LLM frame is available.
645	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
646	
647	# Field-invariant comparator markers (no domain literal): a word window around
648	# these splits the question into compared alternatives, in any field.
649	_FRAME_COMPARATOR_MARKERS_RE = re.compile(
650	    r"\b(versus|vs\.?|compared to|compared with|relative to|against|"
651	    r"as opposed to|rather than)\b",
652	    re.IGNORECASE,
653	)
654	# Field-invariant metric cues: quantity words that suggest a measured outcome.
655	_FRAME_METRIC_CUES_RE = re.compile(
656	    r"\b(rate|ratio|cost|price|percent|percentage|share|level|score|index|"
657	    r"efficiency|yield|throughput|latency|reduction|increase|change|"
658	    r"probability|risk|return|growth|emissions?|temperature|accuracy)\b",
659	    re.IGNORECASE,
660	)
661	# Generic stopwords for content-word entity extraction (no domain terms).
662	_FRAME_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
663	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "is", "are", "was", "were", "of",
664	    "in", "on", "at", "to", "for", "with", "by", "from", "as", "that", "this",
665	    "these", "those", "it", "its", "be", "been", "what", "which", "who", "how",
666	    "why", "when", "where", "we", "our", "their", "between", "into", "about",
667	    "than", "such", "does", "do", "can", "may", "any", "also", "would", "should",
668	    "could", "will", "more", "most", "some", "all", "not", "no", "over", "under",
669	})
670	
671	
672	def extract_research_frame_heuristic(query: str) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
673	    """Field-agnostic frame heuristic for the on-path (brief §2.2).
674	
675	    Returns a dict with the `ResearchFrame` anchor keys (entities / relations /
676	    metrics / comparators / constraints). Pure / no-network / no-LLM and — by
677	    design — uses NO clinical regex. Comparators come from generic comparison
678	    markers, metrics from generic quantity cues, and entities from the
679	    remaining content words. The planner supersedes this when an LLM frame is
680	    available; this is the deterministic seed/fallback.
681	    """
682	    q = (query or "").strip()
683	    frame: dict[str, list[str]] = {
684	        "entities": [],
685	        "relations": [],
686	        "metrics": [],
687	        "comparators": [],
688	        "constraints": [],
689	    }
690	    if not q:
691	        return frame
692	
693	    # Comparators: text fragments flanking a generic comparison marker.
694	    comparators: list[str] = []
695	    for m in _FRAME_COMPARATOR_MARKERS_RE.finditer(q):
696	        tail = q[m.end():].strip()
697	        first_clause = re.split(r"[,.;?]", tail, maxsplit=1)[0].strip()
698	        if first_clause:
699	            comparators.append(first_clause[:60])
700	    frame["comparators"] = list(dict.fromkeys(comparators))[:6]
701	
702	    # Metrics: generic quantity cues present in the question.
703	    metrics = [m.group(1).lower() for m in _FRAME_METRIC_CUES_RE.finditer(q)]
704	    frame["metrics"] = list(dict.fromkeys(metrics))[:8]
705	
706	    # Entities: content words (capitalized phrases or multi-char tokens) minus
707	    # generic stopwords. No domain dictionary.
708	    tokens = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9\-]{2,}", q)
709	    entities: list[str] = []
710	    seen: set[str] = set()
711	    for tok in tokens:
712	        low = tok.lower()
713	        if low in _FRAME_STOPWORDS:
714	            continue
715	        if low in seen:
716	            continue
717	        seen.add(low)
718	        entities.append(tok)
719	    frame["entities"] = entities[:12]
720	    return frame

723	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
724	# B9 domain-generalization (the spine) — cheap domain/intent classifier.
725	#
726	# SG1 fix: POLARIS historically DEFAULTED to clinical and never INFERRED the
727	# domain, so a non-clinical question silently ran the clinical path. This adds a
728	# lightweight, INJECTABLE domain/intent classifier that returns a free-text
729	# domain (NOT a closed enum — avoids re-introducing the closed-set trap one
730	# level up), the deterministic capability flags `is_clinical` / `is_quantitative`,
731	# and `key_entity_types`. It DEGRADES gracefully: a wrong/blank classification
732	# falls to `general` (weight, never abort, never clinical). The output is
733	# ADVISORY routing context — it is NOT serialized into the immutable
734	# `protocol.json`, so adding it does NOT change the protocol bytes/hash (clinical
735	# byte-identity preserved).
736	#
737	# The LLM seam is OPTIONAL + injectable. The DEFAULT is a deterministic, NO-SPEND
738	# heuristic (reuses the `is_clinical_domain` backbone + the field-agnostic frame
739	# heuristic), so build/smoke never hit a live LLM. A caller may inject an LLM
740	# callable (`Callable[[str], str]` returning JSON) to refine the free-text domain
741	# label; an LLM error/garbage output FAILS OPEN to the heuristic (general).
742	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
743	
744	
745	@dataclass(frozen=True)
746	class DomainIntent:
747	    """Advisory domain/intent classification (B9 SG1). NOT serialized into
748	    protocol.json — pure routing context."""
749	
750	    domain: str                  # free-text domain label; "general" when unknown
751	    is_clinical: bool            # the deterministic clinical specialization signal
752	    is_quantitative: bool        # the question asks for a measured quantity
753	    key_entity_types: list[str]  # advisory entity-type hints (free text)
754	    source: str = "heuristic"    # "heuristic" | "llm" — provenance of the label
755	
756	
757	def _heuristic_domain_label(question: str, is_clinical: bool) -> str:
758	    """Deterministic free-text domain label from cheap keyword cues. NO LLM.
759	
760	    Returns a free-text label (NOT a closed enum) — `clinical` when the clinical
761	    signal fired, else the first matching coarse bucket, else `general`. The
762	    label is advisory only; the hard scope-template route uses the SUPPORTED set
763	    with `general` as the safe default, so an unrecognised label never aborts."""
764	    if is_clinical:
765	        return "clinical"
766	    q = (question or "").lower()
767	    # Coarse, additive cue buckets (free text, not a gate). Order = priority.
768	    buckets: list[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]]] = [
769	        ("economics", ("gdp", "inflation", "unemployment", "labor", "labour",
770	                        "wage", "economic", "economy", "productivity", "market",
771	                        "trade", "fiscal", "monetary", "recession")),
772	        ("policy", ("policy", "regulation", "regulatory", "legislation", "bill ",
773	                    "statute", "governance", "compliance", "subsidy", "tariff")),
774	        ("technology", ("ai ", "artificial intelligence", "machine learning",
775	                        "software", "algorithm", "model ", "semiconductor",
776	                        "compute", "neural", "llm")),
777	        ("science", ("physics", "chemistry", "biology", "climate", "emissions",
778	                     "materials", "genome", "experiment", "quantum")),
779	    ]
780	    for label, cues in buckets:
781	        if any(cue in q for cue in cues):
782	            return label
783	    return GENERAL_DOMAIN
784	
785	
786	def _is_quantitative_question(question: str) -> bool:
787	    """Deterministic: does the question ask for a measured quantity? Reuses the
788	    field-agnostic metric-cue regex (no domain literal)."""
789	    return bool(_FRAME_METRIC_CUES_RE.search(question or ""))

792	def classify_domain_intent(
793	    question: str,
794	    *,
795	    evidence: Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]] = None,
796	    domain_hint: Optional[str] = None,
797	    llm: Optional[Any] = None,
798	) -> DomainIntent:
799	    """Classify the question's domain + intent (B9 SG1). Deterministic by
800	    default; an optional injected `llm` callable (``Callable[[str], str]``
801	    returning a JSON object with a ``domain`` key) only REFINES the free-text
802	    label. Never raises, never aborts, FAILS OPEN to `general`.
803	
804	    Args:
805	        question: the raw research question.
806	        evidence: optional evidence rows; used by the deterministic clinical
807	            signal when `domain_hint` is blank.
808	        domain_hint: optional caller-supplied domain (e.g. the sweep's
809	            `q["domain"]`). An explicit non-blank hint is authoritative for the
810	            clinical signal (so `domain="clinical"` -> is_clinical True).
811	        llm: optional injectable seam (``Callable[[str], str]``). Tests inject a
812	            deterministic fake; production may inject a real Writer call. An LLM
813	            error or unparseable output is swallowed -> heuristic label.
814	
815	    Returns:
816	        DomainIntent. `is_clinical` is the deterministic backbone signal;
817	        `domain` is a free-text label degrading to `general`.
818	    """
819	    from src.polaris_graph.domain.domain_signal import (
820	        GENERAL_DOMAIN as _GENERAL,
821	        is_clinical_domain,
822	        normalize_domain,
823	    )
824	    is_clinical = is_clinical_domain(domain_hint, evidence)
825	    is_quant = _is_quantitative_question(question)
826	    frame = extract_research_frame_heuristic(question)
827	    key_entity_types = frame.get("entities", [])[:6]
828	
829	    # Default deterministic label.
830	    label = _heuristic_domain_label(question, is_clinical)
831	    source = "heuristic"
832	
833	    # Optional LLM refinement (fail-open). The LLM may only OVERRIDE the
834	    # free-text label for a non-clinical question; it NEVER overrides the
835	    # deterministic clinical signal (clinical safety stays deterministic).
836	    if llm is not None and not is_clinical:
837	        try:
838	            raw = llm(question)
839	            parsed = json.loads(raw) if isinstance(raw, str) else (raw or {})
840	            llm_domain = normalize_domain(str(parsed.get("domain", "")))
841	            # Codex P2.2: the LLM may refine a NON-clinical free-text label but
842	            # must NEVER set the label to "clinical" when the deterministic
843	            # is_clinical signal is False — clinical routing stays deterministic
844	            # (a clinical label with is_clinical False would be inconsistent and
845	            # could reactivate clinical routing in a downstream consumer).
846	            if (
847	                llm_domain
848	                and llm_domain != _GENERAL
849	                and llm_domain != "clinical"
850	            ):
851	                label = llm_domain
852	                source = "llm"
853	        except Exception:
854	            # Garbage / error -> keep the deterministic heuristic label.
855	            label = _heuristic_domain_label(question, is_clinical)
856	            source = "heuristic"
857	
858	    return DomainIntent(
859	        domain=label or _GENERAL,
860	        is_clinical=is_clinical,
861	        is_quantitative=is_quant,
862	        key_entity_types=list(key_entity_types),
863	        source=source,
864	    )
