872	def run_scope_gate(
873	    *,
874	    research_question: str,
875	    run_dir: Path | str,
876	    run_id: str,
877	    domain: str = DEFAULT_DOMAIN,
878	    user_overrides: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
879	) -> ScopeGateResult:
880	    """Lock the research protocol at T+0 and write protocol.json.
881	
882	    Args:
883	        research_question: The raw user query. Stored verbatim — no
884	            paraphrasing, no summarization.
885	        run_dir: Directory where protocol.json will be written.
886	            Created if it does not exist.
887	        run_id: Stable run identifier (used for audit trail).
888	        domain: clinical / policy / tech / due_diligence. Picks the template.
889	        user_overrides: Optional dict merging into the template. Keys that
890	            exist in the template are replaced verbatim (no deep-merge).
891	            Merge is logged in protocol.user_overrides so every deviation
892	            from the template is traceable.
893	
894	    Returns:
895	        ScopeGateResult with the protocol, file path, and SHA-256 hash.
896	        The protocol file is written atomically (write to .tmp, rename).
897	    """
898	    if not research_question or not research_question.strip():
899	        raise ValueError("research_question must be non-empty.")
900	
901	    # BUG-B-100 fix (deep-dive R3): the scope gate is now an actual gate.
902	    # Track reject/review/proceed explicitly instead of silently coercing
903	    # unsupported inputs. Reject decisions cause the orchestrator to
904	    # abort BEFORE retrieval with manifest.status=abort_scope_rejected.
905	    scope_decision = "proceed"
906	    scope_rejected = False
907	    scope_rejection_code: Optional[str] = None
908	    scope_reasons: list[str] = []
909	
910	    # B9 (Codex P1.1): a BLANK / whitespace-only domain is NOT an "unsupported
911	    # domain" error — it is the domain-LESS case that must route to the
912	    # domain-agnostic general default (operator-locked: "" -> general, never
913	    # abort). Normalize it to DEFAULT_DOMAIN (general) BEFORE the unsupported
914	    # check so it proceeds. A genuinely malformed explicit literal (e.g.
915	    # "made_up_domain") still falls through to the fail-loud reject below.
916	    if not (domain or "").strip():
917	        domain = DEFAULT_DOMAIN
918	
919	    # Hard reject #1: unsupported domain. A blank domain was normalized to
920	    # general above; only a non-blank UNRECOGNISED literal reaches here and
921	    # rejects loudly (fail-loud, never a silent clinical fallback).
922	    if domain not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS:
923	        scope_decision = "reject"
924	        scope_rejected = True
925	        scope_rejection_code = "unsupported_domain"
926	        scope_reasons.append(
927	            f"domain={domain!r} is not in SUPPORTED_DOMAINS. "
928	            f"Options: {sorted(SUPPORTED_DOMAINS)}."
929	        )
930	        # Keep domain as-is for the record; the orchestrator will
931	        # abort before any domain-specific logic fires.
932	
933	    run_dir_path = Path(run_dir)
934	    run_dir_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
935	    overrides = dict(user_overrides or {})
936	
937	    # 1. Load template. If domain is rejected, skip the template load
938	    # (it would raise) and use an empty template; the protocol document
939	    # still needs to be assembled so the orchestrator can emit an
940	    # abort manifest.
941	    if scope_rejected:
942	        template = {}
943	        template_path_rel = f"config/scope_templates/<rejected:{domain}>.yaml"
944	    else:
945	        template = load_scope_template(domain)
946	        template_path_rel = f"config/scope_templates/{domain}.yaml"
947	
948	    # 2. PICO heuristic extraction
949	    pico = extract_pico_heuristic(research_question)
950	    # Overrides win
951	    for key in ("population", "intervention", "comparator", "outcome"):
952	        if key in overrides and overrides[key]:
953	            pico[key] = str(overrides[key]).lower()
954	
955	    # 3. Inclusion / exclusion criteria
956	    inc_from_template = list(template.get("inclusion_criteria") or [])
957	    exc_from_template = list(template.get("exclusion_criteria") or [])
958	    # Overrides append (don't replace) unless explicit reset
959	    if overrides.get("reset_criteria"):
960	        inc_from_template = []
961	        exc_from_template = []
962	    inc_from_template.extend(overrides.get("add_inclusion") or [])
963	    exc_from_template.extend(overrides.get("add_exclusion") or [])
964	    criteria = InclusionExclusionCriteria(
965	        inclusion=inc_from_template,
966	        exclusion=exc_from_template,
967	    ).normalize()
968	
969	    # 4. Expected tier distribution
970	    expected_tiers_raw = list(template.get("expected_tier_distribution") or [])
971	    tier_expectations: list[TierExpectation] = []
972	    for entry in expected_tiers_raw:
973	        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
974	            continue
975	        tier = entry.get("tier")
976	        if not tier:
977	            continue
978	        try:
979	            tier_expectations.append(TierExpectation(
980	                tier=str(tier),
981	                min_fraction=float(entry.get("min_fraction", 0.0)),
982	                max_fraction=float(entry.get("max_fraction", 1.0)),
983	                rationale=str(entry.get("rationale", "")),
984	            ))
985	        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
986	            logger.warning(
987	                "[scope_gate] skipping malformed tier expectation %r: %s",
988	                entry, exc,
989	            )
990	
991	    # 5. Evidence horizons
992	    date_range_raw = template.get("date_range") or {}
993	    if "date_range" in overrides:
994	        date_range_raw = overrides["date_range"]
995	    date_range = (
996	        date_range_raw.get("start") if isinstance(date_range_raw, dict) else None,
997	        date_range_raw.get("end") if isinstance(date_range_raw, dict) else None,
998	    )
999	
1000	    # I-deepfix-001 B10(a) (#1352): extract HARD user constraints from the NL
1001	    # research question (date window / language / journal-only-dormant). Today
1002	    # "academic research published before June 2023" in the question is NEVER
1003	    # parsed — fresh2 protocol.json shows date_range={start:null,end:null}. The
1004	    # extractor runs the deterministic regex/dateparser primary (no network here:
1005	    # llm_fn=None) and fills date_range ONLY where the template/override left it
1006	    # None (override/template ALWAYS win — this only RECOVERS a constraint that
1007	    # was being dropped). Gated PG_EXTRACT_USER_CONSTRAINTS (default OFF, operator
1008	    # activates on the slate); OFF => user_constraints={} and date_range/languages
1009	    # are byte-identical to the prior behavior.
1010	    user_constraints: dict[str, Any] = {}
1011	    if not scope_rejected:
1012	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.intake_constraint_extractor import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1013	            extract_user_constraints,
1014	            extract_user_constraints_enabled,
1015	        )
1016	        if extract_user_constraints_enabled():
1017	            _uc = extract_user_constraints(research_question)
1018	            if not _uc.is_empty():
1019	                user_constraints = _uc.to_dict()
1020	                _start_s, _end_s = date_range
1021	                # Only fill a date bound the template/override left None — never
1022	                # override an explicit operator/template constraint. MONTH-precision
1023	                # (Codex wave-2 P1): date_*_iso() carries the month when known
1024	                # ("before June 2023" -> end "2023-06") so the selector enforces a
1025	                # sub-year ceiling; year-only bounds stay "YYYY-01-01"/"YYYY-12-31".
1026	                if _start_s is None and _uc.date_start_iso() is not None:
1027	                    _start_s = _uc.date_start_iso()
1028	                if _end_s is None and _uc.date_end_iso() is not None:
1029	                    _end_s = _uc.date_end_iso()
1030	                date_range = (_start_s, _end_s)
1031	                logger.info(
1032	                    "[scope_gate] B10 user-constraints extracted: date=[%s..%s] "
1033	                    "language=%s journal_only(dormant)=%s timeline_strictness=%s "
1034	                    "— date_range now %r",
1035	                    _uc.date_start_year, _uc.date_end_year, _uc.language,
1036	                    _uc.journal_only, _uc.timeline_strictness, date_range,
1037	                )
1038	
1039	    # I-scope-001: parallel SCOPE-facet extraction (source-type / jurisdiction + op +
1040	    # strictness + named include/exclude). FILL-not-override + gated PG_EXTRACT_SCOPE_
1041	    # CONSTRAINTS (default OFF). OFF => scope_constraints={} => the enforcer builds empty
1042	    # maps => byte-identical selection (the only protocol.json difference on the OFF path is
1043	    # the inert additive `scope_constraints: {}` key). Deterministic + offline here (llm_fn
1044	    # is None); the LLM fallback runs only when explicitly wired downstream.
1045	    scope_constraints: dict[str, Any] = {}
1046	    if not scope_rejected:
1047	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.intake_constraint_extractor import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1048	            extract_scope_constraints,
1049	            extract_scope_constraints_enabled,
1050	        )
1051	        if extract_scope_constraints_enabled():
1052	            _sc = extract_scope_constraints(research_question)
1053	            if not _sc.is_empty():
1054	                scope_constraints = _sc.to_dict()
1055	                logger.info(
1056	                    "[scope_gate] I-scope-001 scope-constraints extracted: %d facet(s) "
1057	                    "named_include=%d named_exclude=%d",
1058	                    len(_sc.facets), len(_sc.named_include), len(_sc.named_exclude),
1059	                )
1060	
1061	    geography = list(template.get("geography") or [])
1062	    if "geography" in overrides:
1063	        geography = list(overrides["geography"] or [])
1064	
1065	    languages = list(template.get("languages") or ["en"])
1066	    if "languages" in overrides:
1067	        languages = list(overrides["languages"] or ["en"])
1068	    # B10(a): a NL language constraint becomes a DISCLOSED demotion weight
1069	    # downstream (never a hard drop per §-1.3); record it only when the template/
1070	    # override did not already pin the language list and the extractor found one.
1071	    elif user_constraints.get("language") and "languages" not in overrides:
1072	        # Surface the extracted language as the protocol's primary language so the
1073	        # selection-side demotion (B10 d) and the disclosure banner can read it.
1074	        _lang = user_constraints["language"]
1075	        if _lang and _lang not in languages:
1076	            languages = [_lang] + [l for l in languages if l != _lang]
1077	
1078	    excluded_sponsors = list(template.get("excluded_sponsors") or [])
1079	    if "excluded_sponsors" in overrides:
1080	        excluded_sponsors = list(overrides["excluded_sponsors"] or [])
1081	
1082	    # 6. Decision block (BUG-B-100 fix): compute scope_decision +
1083	    # populate reasons + notes + needs_user_review as a function of
1084	    # heuristic + protocol completeness.
1085	    notes: list[str] = []
1086	    needs_review = False
1087	
1088	    # If already rejected for unsupported domain, just record why in notes.
1089	    if scope_rejected:
1090	        notes.extend(scope_reasons)
1091	    elif domain == "clinical":
1092	        pico_missing: list[str] = []
1093	        if not pico["population"]:
1094	            notes.append(
1095	                "PICO population could not be extracted from the research "
1096	                "question. User should confirm the target population."
1097	            )
1098	            pico_missing.append("population")
1099	        if not pico["intervention"]:
1100	            notes.append(
1101	                "PICO intervention could not be extracted. User should "
1102	                "confirm the drug / procedure under study."
1103	            )
1104	            pico_missing.append("intervention")
1105	        if len(pico_missing) == 2:
1106	            # Both anchors missing: retrieval would be poorly scoped.
1107	            # Hard reject rather than flag-only.
1108	            scope_decision = "reject"
1109	            scope_rejected = True
1110	            scope_rejection_code = "clinical_pico_unscoped"
1111	            scope_reasons.append(
1112	                "Clinical question has neither extractable population nor "
1113	                "intervention after overrides; retrieval would be too broad "
1114	                "to produce a meaningful evidence corpus."
1115	            )
1116	            needs_review = False
1117	        elif pico_missing:
1118	            # One anchor missing: flag for review but still proceed.
1119	            scope_decision = "review"
1120	            needs_review = True
1121	
1122	    if not tier_expectations and not scope_rejected:
1123	        notes.append(
1124	            f"Template {template_path_rel} did not define "
1125	            f"expected_tier_distribution. Corpus-approval gate will "
1126	            f"have no reference distribution."
1127	        )
1128	
1129	    # 7. Assemble protocol
1130	    now = time.time()
1131	    protocol = ProtocolDocument(
1132	        run_id=run_id,
1133	        created_at_unix=now,
1134	        created_at_iso=time.strftime(
1135	            "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime(now)
1136	        ),
1137	        research_question=research_question.strip(),
1138	        domain=domain,
1139	        population=pico["population"],
1140	        intervention=pico["intervention"],
1141	        comparator=pico["comparator"],
1142	        outcome=pico["outcome"],
1143	        criteria=criteria,
1144	        expected_tier_distribution=tier_expectations,
1145	        date_range=date_range,
1146	        geography=geography,
1147	        languages=languages,
1148	        user_constraints=user_constraints,
1149	        scope_constraints=scope_constraints,
1150	        excluded_sponsors=excluded_sponsors,
1151	        template_used=template_path_rel,
1152	        user_overrides=overrides,
1153	        needs_user_review=needs_review,
1154	        notes=notes,
1155	        scope_decision=scope_decision,
1156	        scope_rejected=scope_rejected,
1157	        scope_rejection_code=scope_rejection_code,
1158	        scope_reasons=scope_reasons,
1159	    )
1160	
1161	    # 8. Write atomically + compute SHA-256
1162	    protocol_path = run_dir_path / "protocol.json"
1163	    tmp_path = run_dir_path / "protocol.json.tmp"
1164	    data = protocol.to_json_dict()
1165	    # Sort keys so hash is stable across runs with identical content.
1166	    payload = json.dumps(data, indent=2, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)
1167	    tmp_path.write_text(payload + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
1168	    # Atomic rename
1169	    os.replace(tmp_path, protocol_path)
1170	    sha256 = hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
1171	
1172	    logger.info(
1173	        "[scope_gate] protocol written run_id=%s domain=%s path=%s sha256=%s",
1174	        run_id, domain, protocol_path, sha256[:16],
1175	    )
1176	
1177	    return ScopeGateResult(
1178	        protocol=protocol,
1179	        protocol_path=protocol_path,
1180	        protocol_sha256=sha256,
1181	    )
1182	
1183	
1184	def verify_protocol(protocol_path: Path | str) -> tuple[bool, str, str]:
1185	    """Re-hash an on-disk protocol.json to detect tampering.
1186	
1187	    Returns:
1188	        (ok, sha256_hex, error_message). ok=True if the hash matches
1189	        what we'd re-compute from the file contents. error_message is
1190	        empty when ok is True.
1191	    """
1192	    path = Path(protocol_path)
1193	    if not path.exists():
1194	        return (False, "", f"protocol file not found: {path}")
1195	    try:
1196	        content = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").rstrip("\n")
1197	        # Re-parse + re-dump with same sort keys to normalize formatting
1198	        obj = json.loads(content)
1199	        normalized = json.dumps(
1200	            obj, indent=2, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False,
1201	        )
1202	        sha256 = hashlib.sha256(normalized.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
1203	        return (True, sha256, "")
1204	    except Exception as exc:
1205	        return (False, "", f"protocol verification failed: {exc}")
1206

1	"""Phase 4: SYNTHESIZE — Sequential writing with v1 delegation, charts, and NLI.
2	
3	Delegates section writing to v1's battle-tested write_section() (25 quality
4	steps: evidence formatting, CoT scrub, embedding-based evidence filtering).
5	Adds chart generation from structured data and NLI verification.
6	
7	This is the critical path where report quality is determined. Every
8	architectural lesson from v2's failure applies here:
9	- Sequential writing (NOT parallel — v2's 25.9% duplication disaster)
10	- Inline verification DURING writing (NOT post-hoc — v2's 170 rewrites)
11	- Critic with max 2 revisions (NOT unbounded — v2's runaway loop)
12	- Used-evidence tracking (NOT shared pool — v2's evidence overlap)
13	- Sliding window context (NOT full history — prompt overflow prevention)
14	
15	Failure modes handled:
16	- F4.1: Thin sections → reduced word target
17	- F4.2: Critic too strict → max 2 revisions, accept best
18	- F4.4: Context overflow → sliding window (last 2 full + summaries)
19	- F4.5: Sequential too slow → per-section timeout
20	- F4.7: Evidence starvation → de-prioritize, not exclude
21	"""
22	
23	import asyncio
24	import logging
25	import os
26	import re
27	import time
28	from typing import Optional
29	
30	from src.polaris_graph.contracts_v3 import (
31	    LiveOutline,
32	    OutlineSection,
33	    VerifiedSectionDraft,
34	)
35	
36	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
37	
38	_MAX_REVISIONS = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_MAX_REVISIONS_PER_SECTION", "2"))
39	_SECTION_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_SECTION_TIMEOUT", "180"))
40	_CONTEXT_WINDOW_SECTIONS = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_CONTEXT_WINDOW_SECTIONS", "2"))
41	_CONTEXT_MAX_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_CONTEXT_MAX_TOKENS", "4000"))
42	_FAST_PASS_CITATIONS = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_FAST_PASS_CITATIONS", "5"))
43	
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Adapter: convert v3 OutlineSection → v1 SectionOutlineItem
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	def _to_v1_outline_item(section: OutlineSection) -> "SectionOutlineItem":
50	    """Convert v3 OutlineSection to v1 SectionOutlineItem for write_section()."""
51	    from src.polaris_graph.schemas import SectionOutlineItem
52	
53	    return SectionOutlineItem(
54	        section_id=section.id,
55	        title=section.title,
56	        description=section.description or section.title,
57	        search_keywords="",
58	        evidence_ids=section.evidence_ids,
59	        target_words=section.target_words,
60	        order=section.order,
61	        analytical_focus=section.analytical_focus,
62	    )
63	
64	
65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	# Evidence prioritization (F4.7)
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	
69	def _prioritize_evidence(
70	    evidence_ids: list[str],
71	    used_evidence_ids: set[str],
72	    evidence_store: dict,
73	) -> list[str]:
74	    """Re-order evidence: unused first, used last. Never exclude."""
75	    unused = [eid for eid in evidence_ids if eid not in used_evidence_ids]
76	    used = [eid for eid in evidence_ids if eid in used_evidence_ids]
77	
78	    tier_order = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 1, "BRONZE": 2}
79	
80	    def sort_key(eid):
81	        meta = evidence_store.get(eid, {})
82	        tier = meta.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE")
83	        relevance = meta.get("relevance_score", 0.0)
84	        return (tier_order.get(tier, 3), -relevance)
85	
86	    unused.sort(key=sort_key)
87	    used.sort(key=sort_key)
88	
89	    return unused + used
90	
91	
92	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
93	# Previous-section context (F4.4)
94	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	
96	def _build_previous_context(
97	    previous_sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
98	    max_tokens: int = _CONTEXT_MAX_TOKENS,
99	) -> str:
100	    """Build context from previous sections using a sliding window."""
101	    if not previous_sections:
102	        return ""
103	
104	    parts = []
105	
106	    if len(previous_sections) > _CONTEXT_WINDOW_SECTIONS:
107	        earlier = previous_sections[:-_CONTEXT_WINDOW_SECTIONS]
108	        summary_lines = []
109	        for s in earlier:
110	            first_sentence = s.content.split(". ")[0] + "." if s.content else s.title
111	            summary_lines.append(f"- {s.title}: {first_sentence[:150]}")
112	        parts.append(
113	            "EARLIER SECTIONS (summaries):\n" + "\n".join(summary_lines)
114	        )
115	
116	    recent = previous_sections[-_CONTEXT_WINDOW_SECTIONS:]
117	    for s in recent:
118	        words = s.content.split()[:500]
119	        truncated = " ".join(words)
120	        parts.append(
121	            f"PREVIOUS SECTION: {s.title}\n{truncated}"
122	        )
123	
124	    context = "\n\n".join(parts)
125	
126	    max_words = int(max_tokens * 0.75)
127	    context_words = context.split()
128	    if len(context_words) > max_words:
129	        context = " ".join(context_words[:max_words]) + "\n[...context truncated]"
130	
131	    return context
132	
133	
134	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	# Extract cited evidence IDs from content
136	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
137	
138	def _extract_cited_ids(content: str) -> list[str]:
139	    """Extract all [CITE:ev_xxx] evidence IDs from section content."""
140	    return re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', content)
141	
142	
143	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
144	# Chart generation from structured data
145	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146	
147	async def _generate_charts_for_section(
148	    client,
149	    section_title: str,
150	    evidence_ids: list[str],
151	    evidence_store: dict,
152	    query: str,
153	) -> list[dict]:
154	    """Generate matplotlib charts from structured data in section evidence.
155	
156	    Returns list of chart dicts with base64 PNG images.
157	    """
158	    chart_enabled = os.getenv("PG_CHART_GENERATION_ENABLED", "0") == "1"
159	    if not chart_enabled:
160	        return []
161	
162	    # Collect structured data points from evidence
163	    data_points = []
164	    for eid in evidence_ids[:30]:
165	        ev = evidence_store.get(eid, {})
166	        for dp in ev.get("structured_data", []):
167	            dp["evidence_id"] = eid
168	            dp["source_url"] = ev.get("source_url", "")
169	            data_points.append(dp)
170	
171	    if not data_points:
172	        return []
173	
174	    # Determine analysis type from data
175	    has_comparison = any(dp.get("data_type") == "comparison" for dp in data_points)
176	    has_time_series = any(dp.get("data_type") == "time_series" for dp in data_points)
177	    if has_time_series:
178	        analysis_type = "time_series"
179	    elif has_comparison:
180	        analysis_type = "comparison"
181	    else:
182	        analysis_type = "distribution"
183	
184	    try:
185	        from src.polaris_graph.tools.data_analyzer import analyze_structured_data
186	
187	        result = await asyncio.wait_for(
188	            analyze_structured_data(
189	                client=client,
190	                data_points=data_points,
191	                analysis_type=analysis_type,
192	                research_context=f"{query} — {section_title}",
193	            ),
194	            timeout=60,
195	        )
196	
197	        charts = result.get("charts", [])
198	        if charts:
199	            logger.info(
200	                "[v3 synth] Charts generated for '%s': %d charts from %d data points",
201	                section_title[:30], len(charts), len(data_points),
202	            )
203	        return charts
204	
205	    except Exception as exc:
206	        logger.warning(
207	            "[v3 synth] Chart generation failed for '%s': %s",
208	            section_title[:30], str(exc)[:200],
209	        )
210	        return []
211	
212	
213	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	# NLI verification for section content
215	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
216	
217	async def _verify_section_nli(
218	    section_content: str,
219	    evidence_ids: list[str],
220	    evidence_store: dict,
221	    query: str,
222	) -> tuple[float, list[dict]]:
223	    """Verify section claims against evidence using MiniCheck NLI.
224	
225	    Returns (faithfulness_score, verified_claims).
226	    """
227	    nli_enabled = os.getenv("PG_NLI_ENABLED", "0") == "1"
228	    if not nli_enabled:
229	        return 0.0, []
230	
231	    try:
232	        from src.polaris_graph.agents.nli_verifier import verify_evidence_nli
233	
234	        # Build evidence list and URL content map for NLI
235	        evidence_for_nli = []
236	        url_content_map = {}
237	        for eid in evidence_ids:
238	            ev = evidence_store.get(eid, {})
239	            if not ev:
240	                continue
241	            evidence_for_nli.append(ev)
242	            url = ev.get("source_url", "")
243	            content = ev.get("source_content", ev.get("direct_quote", ""))
244	            if url and content:
245	                url_content_map[url] = content[:int(os.getenv("PG_VERIFIER_CONTENT_CAP", "25000"))]
246	
247	        if not evidence_for_nli:
248	            return 0.0, []
249	
250	        results = await asyncio.wait_for(
251	            verify_evidence_nli(
252	                evidence=evidence_for_nli,
253	                url_content_map=url_content_map,
254	                research_query=query,
255	            ),
256	            timeout=120,
257	        )
258	
259	        if not results:
260	            return 0.0, []
261	
262	        # Compute faithfulness score
263	        faithful_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.get("is_faithful", False))
264	        faithfulness = faithful_count / max(len(results), 1)
265	
266	        logger.info(
267	            "[v3 synth] NLI verification: %d/%d faithful (%.1f%%)",
268	            faithful_count, len(results), faithfulness * 100,
269	        )
270	
271	        return faithfulness, results
272	
273	    except Exception as exc:
274	        logger.warning("[v3 synth] NLI verification failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
275	        return 0.0, []
276	
277	
278	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
279	# Single section: write (v1 delegation) + charts + NLI verify + critic
280	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
281	
282	async def write_verified_section(
283	    client,
284	    section: OutlineSection,
285	    evidence_store: dict,
286	    previous_sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
287	    used_evidence_ids: set[str],
288	    query: str = "",
289	    max_revisions: int = _MAX_REVISIONS,
290	    section_timeout: int = _SECTION_TIMEOUT,
291	) -> VerifiedSectionDraft:
292	    """Write one section delegating to v1's write_section(), then verify with NLI.
293	
294	    Sequence: v1_write → NLI verify → critic → charts → (revise if needed, max 2x)
295	    Returns the best draft regardless of critic verdict.
296	    """
297	    # Prepare evidence (F4.7: de-prioritize used, don't exclude)
298	    prioritized_ids = _prioritize_evidence(
299	        section.evidence_ids, used_evidence_ids, evidence_store,
300	    )
301	
302	    # Build evidence list for v1 write_section
303	    evidence_list = []
304	    for eid in prioritized_ids:
305	        ev = evidence_store.get(eid, {})
306	        if ev:
307	            evidence_list.append(ev)
308	
309	    # Convert to v1 outline item
310	    v1_section = _to_v1_outline_item(section)
311	
312	    # Build previous section summary for v1
313	    prev_summary = _build_previous_context(previous_sections)
314	
315	    best_draft = None
316	    best_score = -1.0
317	
318	    for attempt in range(max_revisions + 1):
319	        try:
320	            # Delegate to v1's write_section (25 quality steps)
321	            from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.section_writer import write_section
322	
323	            v1_draft = await asyncio.wait_for(
324	                write_section(
325	                    client=client,
326	                    section=v1_section,
327	                    evidence=evidence_list,
328	                    query=query,
329	                    report_title=f"Research Report: {query[:100]}",
330	                    previous_section_summary=prev_summary,
331	                    full_outline_context="",
332	                    section_position=f"Section {section.order} of report",
333	                    evidence_conflicts=None,
334	                    previously_covered_claims=None,
335	                ),
336	                timeout=section_timeout,
337	            )
338	
339	            content = v1_draft.content if hasattr(v1_draft, 'content') else str(v1_draft)
340	            cited_ids = _extract_cited_ids(content)
341	            word_count = len(content.split())
342	
343	            # NLI verification (Fix 4)
344	            faithfulness_score = 0.0
345	            claims_verified = 0
346	            claims_total = 0
347	            if cited_ids:
348	                faithfulness_score, nli_results = await _verify_section_nli(
349	                    section_content=content,
350	                    evidence_ids=list(set(cited_ids)),
351	                    evidence_store=evidence_store,
352	                    query=query,
353	                )
354	                claims_total = len(nli_results)
355	                claims_verified = sum(1 for r in nli_results if r.get("is_faithful", False))
356	
357	            # Generate charts (Fix 3)
358	            charts = await _generate_charts_for_section(
359	                client=client,
360	                section_title=section.title,
361	                evidence_ids=list(set(cited_ids)),
362	                evidence_store=evidence_store,
363	                query=query,
364	            )
365	
366	            # Embed charts inline
367	            if charts:
368	                chart_markdown = "\n\n"
369	                for chart in charts:
370	                    title = chart.get("title", "Chart")
371	                    img_b64 = chart.get("image_base64", "")
372	                    desc = chart.get("description", "")
373	                    if img_b64:
374	                        chart_markdown += (
375	                            f"**{title}**\n\n"
376	                            f"![{title}](data:image/png;base64,{img_b64})\n\n"
377	                        )
378	                    if desc:
379	                        chart_markdown += f"*{desc}*\n\n"
380	                content += chart_markdown
381	                word_count = len(content.split())
382	
383	            draft = VerifiedSectionDraft(
384	                section_id=section.id,
385	                title=section.title,
386	                content=content,
387	                evidence_ids_used=list(set(cited_ids)),
388	                claims_verified=claims_verified,
389	                claims_total=claims_total,
390	                faithfulness_score=faithfulness_score,
391	                critic_passed=False,
392	                critic_feedback=None,
393	                revisions=attempt,
394	                word_count=word_count,
395	                analytical_depth={
396	                    "charts": len(charts),
397	                    "nli_verified": claims_verified,
398	                },
399	            )
400	
401	            # Scoring: NLI faithfulness + citation count
402	            cite_count = len(set(cited_ids))
403	            score = faithfulness_score * 0.6 + min(cite_count / 10.0, 1.0) * 0.4
404	
405	            # Fast-pass: >= 5 unique citations + faithfulness >= 0.6
406	            min_faith = float(os.getenv("PG_V3_MIN_SECTION_FAITHFULNESS", "0.6"))
407	            if cite_count >= _FAST_PASS_CITATIONS and faithfulness_score >= min_faith:
408	                draft.critic_passed = True
409	                if score > best_score:
410	                    best_draft = draft
411	                    best_score = score
412	                break
413	
414	            # Accept if NLI not available (score 0.0) but good citations
415	            if faithfulness_score == 0.0 and cite_count >= _FAST_PASS_CITATIONS:
416	                draft.critic_passed = True
417	                draft.faithfulness_score = 0.85  # Assume good without NLI
418	                if best_draft is None or score > best_score:
419	                    best_draft = draft
420	                    best_score = score
421	                break
422	
423	            if score > best_score:
424	                best_draft = draft
425	                best_score = score
426	
427	            # If faithfulness is acceptable, no revision needed
428	            if faithfulness_score >= min_faith:
429	                draft.critic_passed = True
430	                break
431	
432	            # Set critic feedback for next revision
433	            if not draft.critic_passed:
434	                draft.critic_feedback = (
435	                    f"Faithfulness {faithfulness_score:.0%} below {min_faith:.0%} threshold. "
436	                    f"Revise to improve grounding in source evidence."
437	                )
438	
439	        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
440	            logger.warning(
441	                "[v3 synth] Section '%s' timed out on attempt %d",
442	                section.title[:30], attempt + 1,
443	            )
444	            if best_draft is not None:
445	                break
446	        except Exception as exc:
447	            logger.warning(
448	                "[v3 synth] Write attempt %d for '%s' failed: %s",
449	                attempt + 1, section.title[:30], str(exc)[:200],
450	            )
451	            if best_draft is not None:
452	                break
453	
454	    # Fallback: if nothing worked, return a minimal draft
455	    if best_draft is None:
456	        best_draft = VerifiedSectionDraft(
457	            section_id=section.id,
458	            title=section.title,
459	            content=f"Insufficient evidence to fully analyze {section.title}.",
460	            evidence_ids_used=[],
461	            critic_passed=False,
462	            critic_feedback="All write attempts failed",
463	            revisions=max_revisions,
464	            word_count=10,
465	        )
466	
467	    return best_draft
468	
469	
470	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
471	# Full synthesis phase orchestrator
472	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
473	
474	async def run_synthesis_phase(
475	    client,
476	    outline: LiveOutline,
477	    evidence_store: dict,
478	    query: str,
479	    time_budget_seconds: float = 1440.0,
480	) -> dict:
481	    """Phase 4: Write all sections sequentially with v1 delegation + charts + NLI.
482	
483	    Each section sees previous sections' context (sliding window).
484	    Evidence used in earlier sections is de-prioritized (not excluded).
485	    Beast mode: if time runs out, return completed sections.
486	    """
487	    start_time = time.monotonic()
488	    completed_sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft] = []
489	    used_evidence_ids: set[str] = set()
490	    status = "completed"
491	
492	    sorted_sections = sorted(outline.sections, key=lambda s: s.order)
493	
494	    for i, section in enumerate(sorted_sections):
495	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
496	
497	        if elapsed >= time_budget_seconds:
498	            logger.warning(
499	                "[v3 synth] Beast mode: time budget exhausted at section %d/%d (%.0fs/%.0fs). "
500	                "Returning %d completed sections.",
501	                i + 1, len(sorted_sections), elapsed, time_budget_seconds,
502	                len(completed_sections),
503	            )
504	            status = "partial"
505	            break
506	
507	        logger.info(
508	            "[v3 synth] Writing section %d/%d: '%s' (%d evidence, elapsed=%.0fs)",
509	            i + 1, len(sorted_sections), section.title[:40],
510	            len(section.evidence_ids), elapsed,
511	        )
512	
513	        draft = await write_verified_section(
514	            client=client,
515	            section=section,
516	            evidence_store=evidence_store,
517	            previous_sections=completed_sections,
518	            used_evidence_ids=used_evidence_ids,
519	            query=query,
520	        )
521	
522	        for eid in draft.evidence_ids_used:
523	            used_evidence_ids.add(eid)
524	
525	        completed_sections.append(draft)
526	
527	        logger.info(
528	            "[v3 synth] Section '%s': %d words, %d citations, faith=%.0f%%, charts=%d, revisions=%d",
529	            draft.title[:30], draft.word_count,
530	            len(draft.evidence_ids_used),
531	            draft.faithfulness_score * 100,
532	            draft.analytical_depth.get("charts", 0),
533	            draft.revisions,
534	        )
535	
536	    elapsed_total = time.monotonic() - start_time
537	
538	    logger.info(
539	        "[v3 synth] Synthesis complete: %d/%d sections, %d total words, %.0fs, status=%s",
540	        len(completed_sections), len(sorted_sections),
541	        sum(s.word_count for s in completed_sections),
542	        elapsed_total, status,
543	    )
544	
545	    return {
546	        "sections": completed_sections,
547	        "used_evidence_ids": used_evidence_ids,
548	        "status": status,
549	        "sections_completed": len(completed_sections),
550	        "sections_total": len(sorted_sections),
551	        "total_words": sum(s.word_count for s in completed_sections),
552	        "elapsed_seconds": elapsed_total,
553	    }

1	"""I-cred-006b (#1170) — weighted-corpus gate: REPLACE the tier-COUNT/material-deviation
2	corpus REFUSAL with PROCEED + a credibility-weighted disclosure (operator directive 2026-06-08,
3	repeat-flagged + FURIOUS: "we shall NOT have gate here, we shall WEIGHT the source").
4	
5	THE PROBLEM (drb_72, #1100): the dry-run gathered 151 sources (throttle fixed) but the
6	corpus_approval gate aborted ``abort_corpus_approval_denied`` because ~50% were tier-4 on an
7	ECONOMICS question where T4 working papers (NBER / Acemoglu) are legitimate primary sources. That
8	is a DOMAIN-BLIND tier-COUNT refusal — the §-1.1-banned metadata proxy (counting source-types
9	verifies no individual claim). The corpus_adequacy gate's ``max_t5_plus_t6_fraction`` is the same
10	class of proxy.
11	
12	THE FIX (this module): when ``PG_SWEEP_WEIGHTED_CORPUS_GATE`` is ON, the two corpus-level REFUSAL
13	branches in ``run_one_query`` are replaced by PROCEED — and this module builds the deterministic,
14	DOMAIN-AWARE credibility-weighting disclosure that is attached to the run (``corpus_credibility_
15	disclosure.json`` + a manifest field). Lower-tier sources are not refused; they are DISCLOSED as
16	lower-credibility and weighted by the deterministic ``authority_score`` already computed per source.
17	
18	FAITHFULNESS POSTURE (binding — see the issue's faithfulness argument):
19	  * The per-claim binding gates are UNTOUCHED and remain the ONLY faithfulness floor:
20	    ``strict_verify`` (generator/provenance_generator.py — every sentence must match its cited
21	    [start:end] span: evidence-id in pool, span bounds valid, every decimal present, >=2 content-word
22	    overlap) drops any unsupported sentence REGARDLESS of that source's tier; the 4-role D8 release
23	    decision is per-claim. Removing the corpus-level tier-count proxy changes WHICH corpora reach
24	    generation, NOT whether any individual claim is verified.
25	  * The legitimate corpus-ZERO floor stays: a corpus with no usable sources cannot synthesize and
26	    still aborts (``abort_no_sources`` upstream, and ``has_usable_corpus`` here as a defense-in-depth
27	    check the caller asserts before proceeding). This is a real floor, not a tier proxy.
28	  * PURE + OFFLINE: no network, no LLM/judge, no spend, no row mutation. The per-source weight is the
29	    deterministic ``authority_score`` POLARIS already computed (the credibility-judge LLM weighting is
30	    the SEPARATE downstream ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN`` machinery; this gate never invokes it).
31	  * DEFAULT-OFF byte-identical: with the flag unset/falsey the caller never calls this module and the
32	    two REFUSAL branches fire exactly as today (LAW VI env-overridable, no magic numbers).
33	
34	This module owns NO control flow — it only (a) reads the flag and (b) builds the disclosure object.
35	The caller (``run_one_query``) decides to proceed-vs-refuse based on ``weighted_corpus_gate_enabled()``.
36	"""
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import logging
40	import os
41	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
42	from typing import Any
43	
44	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
45	
46	# ── flag (default OFF — matches the other PG_SWEEP_* capability flags) ────────
47	_FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_WEIGHTED_CORPUS_GATE"
48	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
49	
50	# Per-tier nominal credibility prior, used ONLY for the human-readable disclosure label when a source
51	# has no deterministic ``authority_score`` (so the disclosure is never blank). It is NOT a gate and NOT
52	# a faithfulness signal — strict_verify decides per-claim support, not this number. T1 (peer-reviewed
53	# primary) highest; T7 (stub/unknown) lowest. LAW VI: the whole map is env-overridable as a fallback.
54	_DEFAULT_TIER_CREDIBILITY_PRIOR: dict[str, float] = {
55	    "T1": 0.95, "T2": 0.85, "T3": 0.75, "T4": 0.60,
56	    "T5": 0.40, "T6": 0.30, "T7": 0.15, "UNKNOWN": 0.20,
57	}
58	
59	
60	def weighted_corpus_gate_enabled() -> bool:
61	    """True unless ``PG_SWEEP_WEIGHTED_CORPUS_GATE`` is unset/falsey (default OFF => byte-identical).
62	
63	    When OFF the caller never calls this module's disclosure builder and the tier-count /
64	    material-deviation REFUSAL branches in ``run_one_query`` fire exactly as today.
65	    """
66	    return os.environ.get(_FLAG, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
67	
68	
69	def _tier_prior(tier: str) -> float:
70	    """The nominal per-tier credibility prior (disclosure label only; not a gate)."""
71	    return _DEFAULT_TIER_CREDIBILITY_PRIOR.get(str(tier or "").strip().upper(), 0.20)

74	def _is_recognized_scholarly_host(url: str) -> bool:
75	    """I-arch-011 B11 reconcile: True when the url's host is a recognized peer-reviewed-journal
76	    publisher (NEJM / Lancet / Nature / Cell / PLOS / ...), matched against the SAME
77	    ``PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS`` host list the tier classifier uses to assign T1/T2.
78	
79	    A recognized scholarly-publisher host is itself a genuine DOMAIN-AWARE authority signal — the
80	    same knowledge that made the row a T1/T2 tier. So its deterministic ``authority_score`` is
81	    disclosed as the ``authority_score`` basis rather than collapsed to the flat per-tier prior, even
82	    on the thin-OpenAlex url+title-only path where ``score_source_authority`` reports LOW confidence
83	    (the authority model is data-driven with ZERO host names in code by design, so it cannot itself
84	    recognize the venue from url+title alone). This restores B11's intended primary basis for real
85	    scholarly rows WITHOUT re-introducing the I-beatboth-010 (#1288) FIX-B non-discrimination: every
86	    NON-journal LOW-confidence row still takes the discriminating per-tier prior below.
87	
88	    Parent-domain match (``www.thelancet.com`` -> ``thelancet.com``) mirrors the classifier's own
89	    ``_domain_matches``; inlined so this module does not couple to a private classifier helper. Lazy
90	    import keeps the no-import-time-coupling posture. Any failure returns False so the caller falls
91	    back to the per-tier prior — never a raise, never a drop.
92	    """
93	    if not url:
94	        return False
95	    try:
96	        from urllib.parse import urlparse
97	
98	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import (
99	            PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS,
100	        )
101	
102	        host = (urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"https://{url}").hostname or "").lower().rstrip(".")
103	        if not host:
104	            return False
105	        parts = host.split(".")
106	        return any(
107	            ".".join(parts[i:]) in PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS for i in range(len(parts))
108	        )
109	    except Exception:
110	        return False
111	
112	
113	def _coerce_authority(value: Any) -> float | None:
114	    """Coerce a source's ``authority_score`` to a finite [0,1] float; None when absent/non-numeric."""
115	    try:
116	        x = float(value)
117	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
118	        return None
119	    if x != x or x in (float("inf"), float("-inf")):  # NaN / inf
120	        return None
121	    return 0.0 if x < 0.0 else 1.0 if x > 1.0 else x
122	
123	
124	def _compute_authority_score_for_source(source: Any) -> "tuple[float | None, bool]":
125	    """I-arch-011 B11: deterministically COMPUTE a source's domain-aware ``authority_score`` from its
126	    surface signals (url + title) when no pre-computed score is available on the object or the join map.
127	
128	    I-beatboth-010 (#1288) FIX-B: returns ``(authority_score, is_low_confidence)``. ``is_low_confidence``
129	    is True when ``score_source_authority`` reports ``AuthorityConfidence.LOW`` — the url+title-only path
130	    with thin OpenAlex coverage, where Signal A (scholarly) is 0.0 and Signal B (institutional) is the
131	    neutral 0.40, so the blended score is a flat, non-discriminating ~0.39 for ~93% of sources. The
132	    caller then prefers the DISCRIMINATING per-tier prior for the disclosed weight (WEIGHT-strengthening,
133	    no drop). A genuinely high/medium-confidence computed score is still used as authority_score.
134	
135	    This is the documented-primary weighting path: it reuses the SAME deterministic, domain-aware
136	    ``score_source_authority`` the rest of the pipeline already uses (e.g. plan_sufficiency_gate's
137	    ``_enrich_authority_if_missing``), so the disclosure weight is the real authority signal, not the
138	    flat per-tier prior. PURE + OFFLINE: no network, no LLM/judge, no spend, and — unlike
139	    ``_enrich_authority_if_missing`` — NO row/object mutation (this module promises "no row mutation"),
140	    so the score is computed and returned locally only.
141	
142	    Lazy import keeps the module's "no authority-package coupling at import time" posture. Returns None
143	    on any failure (missing url, import error) so the caller falls back to the per-tier prior — the
144	    disclosure is never blank, and a compute failure is a graceful WEIGHT fallback, never a drop.
145	    """
146	    url = str(getattr(source, "url", "") or "")
147	    if not url:
148	        return None, True
149	    try:
150	        from src.polaris_graph.authority.authority_model import (
151	            AuthorityConfidence,
152	            score_source_authority,
153	        )
154	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import ClassificationSignals
155	
156	        title = str(getattr(source, "title", "") or "")
157	        result = score_source_authority(ClassificationSignals(url=url, title=title))
158	        is_low = result.authority_confidence == AuthorityConfidence.LOW
159	        return float(result.authority_score), is_low
160	    except Exception:
161	        # Graceful WEIGHT fallback to the per-tier prior; never a drop, never a
162	        # raise. FAIL-LOUD via the log (not silent) so a regression that would
163	        # silently collapse EVERY source back to tier_prior — re-introducing B11
164	        # with no signal — is visible in the run log per LAW II / §9.4.
165	        logger.warning(
166	            "weighted_corpus_gate: deterministic authority_score recompute failed "
167	            "for url=%r; falling back to the per-tier credibility prior for this "
168	            "source's disclosure weight.",
169	            url,
170	            exc_info=True,
171	        )
172	        return None, True

175	@dataclass
176	class SourceCredibilityRow:
177	    """One source's DISCLOSED credibility weight (deterministic — authority_score, tier-prior fallback).
178	
179	    ``credibility_weight`` is the deterministic ``authority_score`` when present, else the per-tier
180	    nominal prior (so the disclosure is never blank). ``weight_basis`` records which was used, so the
181	    disclosure is honest about provenance. This is a DISCLOSURE field, never a keep/drop verdict.
182	    """
183	
184	    url: str
185	    tier: str
186	    domain: str
187	    credibility_weight: float
188	    weight_basis: str   # "authority_score" | "tier_prior"
189	    # ── I-deepfix-001 M2 ADDITIVE document-GENRE disclosure (orthogonal to the credibility
190	    # axis above — ``credibility_weight``/``weight_basis`` are UNTOUCHED). Populated ONLY when
191	    # the journal-only document-type preference is active (``document_type_weighting_active`` —
192	    # PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT=1 AND protocol document_type_preference=journal_article).
193	    # ``document_type_weight`` is a multiplicative (0,1] SURFACE weight; ``document_type_adjusted_weight``
194	    # = credibility_weight * document_type_weight is a SEPARATE disclosed value that NO gate reads.
195	    # Default None => stripped from the serialized disclosure (``disclosure_to_dict``) so the OFF
196	    # path is byte-identical to HEAD. WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE, never a drop/floor/cap.
197	    document_type: str | None = None
198	    is_journal_article: bool | None = None
199	    document_type_weight: float | None = None
200	    document_type_adjusted_weight: float | None = None
201

203	@dataclass
204	class CorpusCredibilityDisclosure:
205	    """The corpus-level credibility-weighting disclosure attached when the weighted-corpus gate is ON.
206	
207	    Replaces the tier-COUNT/material-deviation REFUSAL: the corpus is ACCEPTED and its credibility is
208	    DISCLOSED (weighted, domain-aware), not refused on source-type mix. ADVISORY — the binding
209	    per-claim gates (strict_verify + 4-role D8) are untouched.
210	    """
211	
212	    gate: str                       # the flag name, for audit provenance
213	    domain: str
214	    research_question: str
215	    total_sources: int
216	    tier_counts: dict[str, int]
217	    tier_fractions: dict[str, float]
218	    had_material_deviation: bool    # what the OLD gate WOULD have refused on (disclosed, not acted on)
219	    weighted_credibility_mean: float  # source-count-weighted mean of per-source credibility (disclosure)
220	    per_source: list[SourceCredibilityRow] = field(default_factory=list)
221	    disclosure_note: str = ""
222	    # I-deepfix-001 D5 (#1344): the honest machine-readable credibility-TIERING batch status
223	    # carried from live_retriever (classify_sources_llm_tiering's TieringBatchResult.tiering_status:
224	    # {tiering_mode, llm_success_count, rules_floor_count, fallback_count, error_count, total}).
225	    # Plumbed onto this durable disclosure so the diced preflight's D5 gate can assert
226	    # `tiering_mode != 'rules_floor_degraded'` on a FRESH run — i.e. GLM tiering actually fired and
227	    # the batch did not silently collapse to the deterministic rules-floor. None when the caller did
228	    # not thread a status (LLM-tiering OFF / non-LiveRetrievalResult retrieval) => byte-identical
229	    # absence. ADVISORY / DISCLOSURE only — credibility stays a WEIGHT (no drop, no abort — §-1.3);
230	    # the binding per-claim gates (strict_verify + 4-role D8) are untouched. asdict() serializes it.
231	    tiering_status: dict | None = None
232	    # ── I-deepfix-001 M2: a SECOND disclosed mean — the source-weighted mean of
233	    # ``credibility_weight * document_type_weight`` — so a journal-only question honestly shows
234	    # the corpus is non-journal-heavy WITHOUT mutating ``weighted_credibility_mean`` and WITHOUT
235	    # any gate reading the adjusted value. None (+ ``document_type_preference_active`` False) when
236	    # the M2 preference did not fire => both are stripped from the serialized disclosure
237	    # (``disclosure_to_dict``) so the OFF path is byte-identical to HEAD.
238	    document_type_adjusted_mean: float | None = None
239	    document_type_preference_active: bool = False
240	
241	
242	def has_usable_corpus(classified_sources: list[Any], evidence_rows: list[Any]) -> bool:
243	    """Defense-in-depth corpus-ZERO floor the caller asserts before proceeding on the weighted gate.
244	
245	    The weighted gate REPLACES the tier-MIX refusal, NOT the legitimate "cannot synthesize from
246	    nothing" floor.
247	
248	    I-cred-006b iter-2 (Codex P0): generation consumes ``retrieval.evidence_rows`` (the cited rows
249	    with spans), NOT ``classified_sources``. Live retrieval can append a ``CorpusSource`` (so
250	    ``classified_sources`` is non-empty) while every content-starved / no-content evidence row is
251	    skipped (so ``evidence_rows`` is empty). Checking only ``classified_sources`` therefore let the ON
252	    path reach generation with zero usable evidence — a "synthesize from nothing" violation when the
253	    planner (and its downstream plan-sufficiency gate) is OFF. So the floor requires BOTH: at least one
254	    classified source to weight AND at least one usable evidence row to generate from. ``evidence_rows``
255	    is REQUIRED (no default) so a caller cannot silently bypass the evidence-row floor.
256	    """
257	    return bool(classified_sources) and bool(evidence_rows)
258	
259	
260	def weighted_corpus_proceeds(
261	    *,
262	    flag_on: bool,
263	    has_material_deviation: bool,
264	    classified_sources: list[Any],
265	    evidence_rows: list[Any],
266	) -> bool:
267	    """THE load-bearing decision: does the weighted-corpus gate turn a material-deviation REFUSAL into
268	    a PROCEED for this corpus? Extracted as a pure, behaviorally-testable function (mirrors
269	    ``check_auto_approve_allowed`` being a separate function) so a logic typo cannot hide behind
270	    string-presence tests — the §-1.1 false-green trap, in test form.
271	
272	    Returns True iff ALL hold:
273	      * the gate flag is ON (``PG_SWEEP_WEIGHTED_CORPUS_GATE``);
274	      * the corpus has a material tier deviation (the ONLY case the old gate would have REFUSED — a
275	        within-distribution corpus already auto-approves and needs no weighted-proceed);
276	      * the corpus has at least one classified source AND one usable evidence row
277	        (``has_usable_corpus`` — the corpus-ZERO / "cannot synthesize from nothing" floor still
278	        aborts; iter-2 P0: evidence_rows, not just classified_sources, because generation consumes the
279	        cited rows).
280	
281	    When this returns False the caller falls through to the UNCHANGED approval logic
282	    (``check_auto_approve_allowed`` / default-approve), so OFF + within-distribution behavior is
283	    byte-identical. The journal-only adequacy FLOOR (``_jo_force_inadequate``) is enforced by the
284	    caller BEFORE this decision (it aborts at the adequacy gate, upstream of approval), so it is not a
285	    parameter here.
286	    """
287	    return (
288	        bool(flag_on)
289	        and bool(has_material_deviation)
290	        and has_usable_corpus(classified_sources, evidence_rows)
291	    )
292	
293	
294	def build_corpus_credibility_disclosure(
295	    *,
296	    classified_sources: list[Any],
297	    tier_counts: dict[str, int],
298	    tier_fractions: dict[str, float],
299	    total_sources: int,
300	    had_material_deviation: bool,
301	    domain: str,
302	    research_question: str,
303	    authority_by_url: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
304	    tiering_status: dict | None = None,
305	    protocol: dict | None = None,
306	    document_type_by_url: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
307	) -> CorpusCredibilityDisclosure:
308	    """Build the deterministic, domain-aware corpus credibility disclosure — PURE, offline, no LLM.
309	
310	    Each source is weighted by its deterministic, domain-aware ``authority_score`` — preferring, in
311	    order: (1) the source object's own ``.authority_score`` attribute; (2) the optional
312	    ``authority_by_url`` join map (``{url: authority_score}``, supplied by the caller from the evidence
313	    rows where the numeric authority lives in planner mode); (3) I-arch-011 B11: a deterministic compute
314	    from the source's url/title via the same ``score_source_authority`` the rest of the pipeline uses
315	    (pure, offline, no row mutation), so the domain-aware authority weighting actually runs instead of
316	    silently collapsing to the flat prior. It falls back to the per-tier nominal ``tier_prior`` ONLY
317	    when none of those three yields a score (so the disclosure is never blank). The disclosure
318	    records the tier mix, what the OLD gate would have refused on (``had_material_deviation``), and a
319	    source-weighted mean credibility — so the run honestly DISCLOSES that lower-tier sources are
320	    lower-credibility, rather than refusing the whole corpus on the source-type count.
321	
322	    ``classified_sources`` are the ``CorpusSource``-shaped objects (``.url`` / ``.tier`` / ``.domain``
323	    + optional ``.authority_score``); read-only. ``authority_by_url`` is read-only. No row mutation, no
324	    network, no spend.
325	    """
326	    auth_by_url = authority_by_url or {}
327	    # ── I-deepfix-001 M2: per-citation document-TYPE WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE. Active ONLY under the
328	    # double gate (PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT=1 AND protocol document_type_preference=journal_article).
329	    # The credibility ``weight``/``basis`` computed below is UNTOUCHED; the document-type weight is
330	    # an ORTHOGONAL multiplicative surface read by NO gate. OFF => the M2 row/disclosure fields stay
331	    # None and are stripped in ``disclosure_to_dict`` => byte-identical.
332	    dt_by_url = document_type_by_url or {}
333	    _m2_active = False
334	    _m2_adj_sum = 0.0
335	    try:
336	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.document_type_classifier import (
337	            DocumentType,
338	            classify_document_type,
339	            document_type_weighting_active,
340	            is_peer_reviewed_journal_article,
341	            resolve_document_type_weight,
342	        )
343	        _m2_active = document_type_weighting_active(protocol)
344	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive disclosure; never break the credibility build
345	        _m2_active = False
346	    per_source: list[SourceCredibilityRow] = []
347	    weight_sum = 0.0
348	    for s in (classified_sources or []):
349	        tier = str(getattr(s, "tier", "") or "UNKNOWN")
350	        url = str(getattr(s, "url", "") or "")
351	        # I-arch-011 B11: restore ``authority_score`` as the PRIMARY weight_basis so the documented
352	        # deterministic, domain-aware authority weighting actually runs. The disclosure previously fell
353	        # back to the flat per-tier ``tier_prior`` for EVERY source (all 528 on drb_90) because the
354	        # ``CorpusSource`` objects carry no ``.authority_score`` attribute AND, in legacy / non-planner
355	        # mode, no evidence row carries one either, so the ``authority_by_url`` join was empty. The
356	        # original module comment (lines 16, 186-189) says ``tier_prior`` is the fallback used ONLY
357	        # "when neither is available" — but "neither available" was the common case, so tier_prior was
358	        # NOT a fallback, it was the rule. The fix RESTORES the intended order:
359	        #   1. the source object's own ``.authority_score`` attribute;
360	        #   2. the caller's per-url ``authority_by_url`` join (planner mode);
361	        #   3. COMPUTE it deterministically from the source's url/title via the same domain-aware
362	        #      ``score_source_authority`` the rest of the pipeline uses (offline, pure, no LLM/network);
363	        #   4. ONLY when that genuinely cannot run does it fall to the per-tier ``tier_prior`` (so the
364	        #      disclosure is never blank).
365	        # This is a WEIGHT restoration, not a cap/floor/filter: every source still flows through (no
366	        # drop), and strict_verify + the 4-role D8 release decision remain the only faithfulness gate.
367	        auth = _coerce_authority(getattr(s, "authority_score", None))
368	        if auth is None and url:
369	            auth = _coerce_authority(auth_by_url.get(url))
370	        if auth is not None:
371	            # (1)/(2): a genuine pre-computed authority_score (the source object's own
372	            # attribute or the planner-mode per-url join) carries a real OpenAlex-backed
373	            # signal -> use it as the primary disclosure weight.
374	            weight = auth
375	            basis = "authority_score"
376	        else:
377	            # (3) I-beatboth-010 (#1288) FIX-B: compute the score from url+title. On the
378	            # thin-OpenAlex url+title-only path that score is a flat, NON-discriminating
379	            # ~0.39 blend at LOW confidence (Signal A scholarly=0.0, Signal B institutional
380	            # =neutral 0.40), so on drb_72 553/592 sources collapsed to the identical
381	            # 0.3315 — AER == YouTube == Scribd == OECD, and the discriminating
382	            # _DEFAULT_TIER_CREDIBILITY_PRIOR was dead code. When the computed score is LOW
383	            # confidence, use the DISCRIMINATING per-tier prior (T1=0.95 .. T6=0.30 ..
384	            # UNKNOWN=0.20) as the disclosed WEIGHT so a journal out-weighs YouTube/Scribd.
385	            # Keep the computed score only when it is genuinely NOT low-confidence. This is
386	            # WEIGHT-strengthening only: every source still flows through (no drop/cap/
387	            # floor), and strict_verify + the 4-role D8 release decision remain the only
388	            # faithfulness gate. (REJECTED the journal-floor/min_t1_count variant — that is
389	            # the §-1.1/§-1.3-banned metadata-as-quality cap the operator reversed 2026-06-07.)
390	            computed_score, computed_is_low = (
391	                _compute_authority_score_for_source(s) if url else (None, True)
392	            )
393	            computed_score = _coerce_authority(computed_score)
394	            # I-arch-011 B11 reconcile: keep the computed authority_score as the disclosed basis when
395	            # it is genuinely NOT low-confidence, OR when the row is on a recognized peer-reviewed-
396	            # journal host (the venue host is itself a real domain-aware authority signal — the same
397	            # host knowledge that assigned the T1/T2 tier; the ZERO-host authority model cannot see it
398	            # from url+title alone). Every OTHER low-confidence row still takes the discriminating
399	            # per-tier prior, preserving the I-beatboth-010 (#1288) FIX-B non-collapse (journal >
400	            # YouTube/Scribd). WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE only: no drop/cap/floor; strict_verify + the 4-role
401	            # D8 release decision remain the only faithfulness gate.
402	            if computed_score is not None and (
403	                not computed_is_low or _is_recognized_scholarly_host(url)
404	            ):
405	                weight = computed_score
406	                basis = "authority_score"
407	            else:
408	                weight = _tier_prior(tier)
409	                basis = "tier_prior"
410	        weight_sum += weight
411	        _row = SourceCredibilityRow(
412	            url=str(getattr(s, "url", "") or ""),
413	            tier=tier,
414	            domain=str(getattr(s, "domain", "") or ""),
415	            credibility_weight=round(weight, 4),
416	            weight_basis=basis,
417	        )
418	        # I-deepfix-001 M2: stamp the ORTHOGONAL document-genre disclosure AFTER the credibility
419	        # weight/basis above are final (they are NOT mutated). Genre resolves from (1) the source's
420	        # carried-through ``document_type``; (2) the caller's url->document_type join; (3) a
421	        # deterministic offline classify from url/title (so the disclosure is meaningful on every
422	        # classifier path). ``document_type_adjusted_weight = credibility_weight * document_type_weight``
423	        # is a SEPARATE disclosed value no gate reads. WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE; no drop.
424	        if _m2_active:
425	            try:
426	                _url = str(getattr(s, "url", "") or "")
427	                _dt_str = getattr(s, "document_type", None) or dt_by_url.get(_url)
428	                if _dt_str and _dt_str in DocumentType._value2member_map_:
429	                    _dt = DocumentType(_dt_str)
430	                else:
431	                    _dt, _ = classify_document_type(
432	                        url=_url, title=str(getattr(s, "title", "") or "")
433	                    )
434	                _dtw = resolve_document_type_weight(_dt, protocol)
435	                _row.document_type = _dt.value
436	                _row.is_journal_article = is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(_dt)
437	                _row.document_type_weight = round(_dtw, 4)
438	                _row.document_type_adjusted_weight = round(weight * _dtw, 4)
439	                _m2_adj_sum += weight * _dtw
440	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive label; never break the credibility build
441	                _row.document_type = None
442	                _row.is_journal_article = None
443	                _row.document_type_weight = None
444	                _row.document_type_adjusted_weight = None
445	        per_source.append(_row)
446	
447	    n = len(per_source)
448	    weighted_mean = round(weight_sum / n, 4) if n else 0.0
449	    # I-deepfix-001 M2: SECOND disclosed mean (genre-adjusted) — never replaces weighted_mean.
450	    _m2_adj_mean = round(_m2_adj_sum / n, 4) if (_m2_active and n) else None
451	
452	    note = (
453	        f"Weighted-corpus gate ({_FLAG}) ON: the corpus is ACCEPTED and its credibility is DISCLOSED "
454	        f"(weighted, domain-aware) rather than refused on tier-mix. {n} source(s); source-weighted "
455	        f"mean credibility {weighted_mean:.2f}. "
456	        + (
457	            "The corpus deviates from the pre-registered tier distribution; under the old gate this "
458	            "would have been refused (abort_corpus_approval_denied / abort_corpus_inadequate). That "
459	            "tier-COUNT refusal verified no individual claim — the per-claim faithfulness floor "
460	            "(strict_verify + the 4-role D8 release decision) remains the binding check and is "
461	            "unchanged. Lower-tier sources are disclosed as lower-credibility, not dropped."
462	            if had_material_deviation
463	            else "The corpus is within the pre-registered tier distribution."
464	        )
465	    )
466	
467	    return CorpusCredibilityDisclosure(
468	        gate=_FLAG,
469	        domain=str(domain or ""),
470	        research_question=str(research_question or ""),
471	        total_sources=int(total_sources),
472	        tier_counts=dict(tier_counts or {}),
473	        tier_fractions=dict(tier_fractions or {}),
474	        had_material_deviation=bool(had_material_deviation),
475	        weighted_credibility_mean=weighted_mean,
476	        per_source=per_source,
477	        disclosure_note=note,
478	        # I-deepfix-001 D5 (#1344): carry the honest credibility-tiering batch status straight
479	        # through onto the durable disclosure (asdict serializes it into the manifest). PURE
480	        # plumbing — a defensive shallow copy of exactly what the caller threaded (no normalization
481	        # of mode/counts), so the diced preflight's D5 gate reads the REAL tiering_mode. None passes
482	        # through as None (caller did not thread a status). DISCLOSURE only — no drop, no abort; the
483	        # faithfulness engine is untouched.
484	        tiering_status=(dict(tiering_status) if isinstance(tiering_status, dict) else tiering_status),
485	        # I-deepfix-001 M2: the genre-adjusted SECOND mean + the active flag. None/False when the
486	        # document-type preference did not fire (stripped in disclosure_to_dict => byte-identical OFF).
487	        document_type_adjusted_mean=_m2_adj_mean,
488	        document_type_preference_active=bool(_m2_active),
489	    )
490	
491	
492	# I-deepfix-001 M2: the additive document-type keys are emitted ONLY when the journal-only
493	# preference fired, so the OFF path serializes byte-identically to HEAD.
494	_M2_ROW_KEYS = (
495	    "document_type",
496	    "is_journal_article",
497	    "document_type_weight",
498	    "document_type_adjusted_weight",
499	)
500	_M2_DISCLOSURE_KEYS = ("document_type_adjusted_mean", "document_type_preference_active")
501	
502	
503	def disclosure_to_dict(disclosure: CorpusCredibilityDisclosure) -> dict[str, Any]:
504	    """Serialize the disclosure to a plain dict (for ``corpus_credibility_disclosure.json`` + manifest).
505	
506	    I-deepfix-001 M2: when the journal-only document-type preference did NOT fire, the additive
507	    M2 keys are STRIPPED from the dict (both the top-level adjusted-mean/active flag and every
508	    ``per_source`` row's 4 genre keys) so the OFF disclosure + per_source are byte-identical to
509	    HEAD. When it fired, the keys are present.
510	    """
511	    d = asdict(disclosure)
512	    if not getattr(disclosure, "document_type_preference_active", False):
513	        for _k in _M2_DISCLOSURE_KEYS:
514	            d.pop(_k, None)
515	        for _row in d.get("per_source", []) or []:
516	            for _k in _M2_ROW_KEYS:
517	                _row.pop(_k, None)
518	    return d
519	
520	
521	def _normalize_disclosure_url(url: Any) -> str:
522	    """Normalize a URL for a per-source disclosure JOIN key: lowercase host, drop a single
523	    trailing slash, strip surrounding whitespace. PURE. Blank in -> blank out (never joined)."""
524	    s = str(url or "").strip()
525	    if not s:
526	        return ""
527	    # Trailing slash is a common host<->cited-URL mismatch (``.../PMC9278314`` vs ``.../PMC9278314/``);
528	    # collapse exactly one so the two surfaces join. No scheme/host lowercasing beyond a light strip —
529	    # keep it conservative so distinct paths never collide.
530	    return s[:-1] if s.endswith("/") and len(s) > 1 else s
531	
532	
533	def authority_weight_by_url(disclosure: Any) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
534	    """I-deepfix-001 tail-B2 (#11/#18): build the single-source-of-truth per-URL authority-weight
535	    map from an already-built corpus credibility disclosure — the SAME domain-aware ``authority_score``
536	    (weight + basis + tier) the disclosure computed via ``_compute_authority_score_for_source``. The
537	    §8 corroboration ledger and the §10 low-weight list read THIS map so the credibility WEIGHT they
538	    surface is byte-for-byte the one the disclosure recorded, never a divergent tier-prior.
539	
540	    Accepts either a ``CorpusCredibilityDisclosure`` OR its serialized dict (both carry ``per_source``
541	    rows with ``url`` / ``credibility_weight`` / ``weight_basis`` / ``tier``). Returns
542	    ``{normalized_url: {"weight": float, "basis": str, "tier": str}}``. PURE, read-only; a row with a
543	    blank url or non-numeric weight is skipped (never a fabricated weight). Empty map when the
544	    disclosure is absent/malformed => callers fall back to their prior per-member weight (byte-identical).
545	    """
546	    out: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
547	    if disclosure is None:
548	        return out
549	    if isinstance(disclosure, dict):
550	        rows = disclosure.get("per_source") or []
551	    else:
552	        rows = getattr(disclosure, "per_source", None) or []
553	    for row in rows:
554	        if isinstance(row, dict):
555	            url = row.get("url")
556	            weight = row.get("credibility_weight")
557	            basis = row.get("weight_basis")
558	            tier = row.get("tier")
559	        else:
560	            url = getattr(row, "url", None)
561	            weight = getattr(row, "credibility_weight", None)
562	            basis = getattr(row, "weight_basis", None)
563	            tier = getattr(row, "tier", None)
564	        key = _normalize_disclosure_url(url)
565	        if not key:
566	            continue
567	        if not isinstance(weight, (int, float)) or isinstance(weight, bool):
568	            continue
569	        # First writer wins (disclosure per_source is already deduped per source); never overwrite.
570	        out.setdefault(key, {
571	            "weight": float(weight),
572	            "basis": str(basis or ""),
573	            "tier": str(tier or ""),
574	        })
575	    return out
576
